- `pypdfbox/xmpbox/type/abstract_field.py`: added XMPBox camelCase aliases for `Attribute` and `AbstractField` metadata/attribute accessors, including `getName`, `setName`, `getNamespace`, `setNsURI`, `getValue`, `setValue`, `getPropertyName`, `setPropertyName`, `setAttribute`, `containsAttribute`, `getAttribute`, `getAllAttributes`, `removeAttribute`, and `getMetadata`.

## Wave 61 — COS scalar Java aliases

- `pypdfbox/cos/cos_integer.py`: added PDFBox camelCase aliases `getValue()`, `isValid()`, and `setValid(...)`.
- `pypdfbox/cos/cos_float.py`: added PDFBox camelCase aliases `getValue()`, `getOriginalForm()`, and `setValue(...)`.
- `pypdfbox/cos/cos_string.py`: added PDFBox camelCase aliases `getBytes()`, `getString()`, `isForceHexForm()`, `setForceHexForm(...)`, and `toHexString()`.

## Wave 62 — COSDocument Java aliases

- `pypdfbox/cos/cos_document.py`: added PDFBox camelCase aliases for object-pool, trailer/catalog, encryption, xref, version, and xref-stream accessors (`getObjectFromPool`, `getObject`, `getObjects`, `removeObject`, `addXRefTable`, `getXrefTable`, `getTrailer`, `setTrailer`, `getCatalog`, `isEncrypted`, `getEncryptionDictionary`, `getVersion`, `setVersion`, `isXRefStream`, and `setXRefStream`).

## Wave 63 — NameRecord Java aliases

- `pypdfbox/fontbox/ttf/name_record.py`: added FontBox camelCase aliases for name-record metadata (`getPlatformId` / `setPlatformId`, `getPlatformEncodingId` / `setPlatformEncodingId`, `getLanguageId` / `setLanguageId`, `getNameId` / `setNameId`, `getStringLength` / `setStringLength`, `getStringOffset` / `setStringOffset`, and `getString` / `setString`).

## Wave 64 — XMP structured-type Java aliases

- `pypdfbox/xmpbox/type/job_type.py`: added XMPBox camelCase aliases for job fields (`getId` / `setId`, `getName` / `setName`, and `getUrl` / `setUrl`).
- `pypdfbox/xmpbox/type/resource_event_type.py`: added XMPBox camelCase aliases for resource-event fields (`getAction` / `setAction`, `getChanged` / `setChanged`, `getInstanceID` / `setInstanceID`, `getParameters` / `setParameters`, `getSoftwareAgent` / `setSoftwareAgent`, and `getWhen` / `setWhen`).

## Wave 65 — COSArray Java aliases

- `pypdfbox/cos/cos_array.py`: added PDFBox camelCase aliases for collection, typed value, and conversion helpers, including `addAll`, `getObject`, `removeObject`, `removeAll`, `retainAll`, `growToSize`, `getInt` / `setInt`, `getName` / `setName`, `getFloat` / `setFloat`, `getBoolean` / `setBoolean`, `getString` / `setString`, `setFloatArray`, `toFloatArray`, and the `toCOS...List` conversion helpers.

## Wave 66 — COSDictionary typed Java aliases

- `pypdfbox/cos/cos_dictionary.py`: added PDFBox camelCase aliases for dictionary collection and typed helpers, including `isEmpty`, `keySet`, `entrySet`, `addAll`, `getName` / `setName`, `getString` / `setString`, `getInt` / `setInt`, `getLong` / `setLong`, `getFloat` / `setFloat`, and `getBoolean` / `setBoolean`.

## Wave 67 — CmapSubtable Java aliases

- `pypdfbox/fontbox/ttf/cmap_subtable.py`: added FontBox camelCase aliases for cmap metadata and lookups, including `getPlatformId` / `setPlatformId`, `getPlatformEncodingId` / `setPlatformEncodingId`, `getGlyphId`, `getGlyphIdUVS`, `hasUVS`, `getCharCode`, and `getCharCodes`.

## Wave 68 — XMP dimensions and layer Java aliases

- `pypdfbox/xmpbox/type/dimensions_type.py`: added XMPBox camelCase aliases for dimensions fields (`getH` / `setH`, `getW` / `setW`, and `getUnit` / `setUnit`).
- `pypdfbox/xmpbox/type/layer_type.py`: added XMPBox camelCase aliases for Photoshop layer fields and typed properties (`getLayerName` / `setLayerName`, `getLayerText` / `setLayerText`, `getLayerNameProperty` / `setLayerNameProperty`, and `getLayerTextProperty` / `setLayerTextProperty`).

## Wave 69 — XMP version Java aliases

- `pypdfbox/xmpbox/type/version_type.py`: added XMPBox camelCase aliases for version fields (`getComments` / `setComments`, `getEvent` / `setEvent`, `getModifier` / `setModifier`, `getModifyDate` / `setModifyDate`, and `getVersion` / `setVersion`).

## Wave 70 — XMP resource reference Java aliases

- `pypdfbox/xmpbox/type/resource_ref_type.py`: added XMPBox camelCase aliases for resource-reference fields, including document/instance IDs, file path, management fields, part/rendition fields, last-modify date, and alternate-path helpers.

## Wave 199 — PDType1Font path / width / Standard 14 constants round-out

- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/font/pd_type1_font.py`:
  - Added 14 PostScript ``BaseFont`` string constants directly on ``PDType1Font`` (``HELVETICA``, ``HELVETICA_BOLD``, ``HELVETICA_OBLIQUE``, ``HELVETICA_BOLD_OBLIQUE``, ``TIMES_ROMAN``, ``TIMES_BOLD``, ``TIMES_ITALIC``, ``TIMES_BOLD_ITALIC``, ``COURIER``, ``COURIER_BOLD``, ``COURIER_OBLIQUE``, ``COURIER_BOLD_OBLIQUE``, ``SYMBOL``, ``ZAPF_DINGBATS``) — values match upstream ``Standard14Fonts.FontName`` ``getName()`` exactly so callers can spell ``PDType1Font.HELVETICA`` ergonomically without reaching for the upstream enum.
  - Added ``SUBSTITUTE_NOTDEF_WIDTH = 250.0`` named constant for the PDFBOX-1900 sentinel previously hardcoded inline by upstream's ``getWidthFromFont``.
  - ``get_path(name)`` now mirrors upstream ``PDType1Font.getPath(String)`` — short-circuits to ``[]`` for ``.notdef`` on non-embedded fonts (PDFBOX-2421) and routes the program lookup through ``get_name_in_font`` so ``ALT_NAMES`` ligature fallbacks (``ff`` → ``f_f``, ArialMT ``ellipsis`` → ``elipsis``) reach the program.
  - Added ``get_path_for_code(code)`` mirroring upstream ``PDType1Font.getPath(int)`` — encoding-keyed path lookup.
  - Added ``get_normalized_path_for_code(code)`` mirroring upstream ``PDType1Font.getNormalizedPath(int)`` — falls back to the ``.notdef`` glyph path when the primary lookup yields ``[]``.
  - Added ``get_width_from_font(code)`` mirroring upstream ``PDType1Font.getWidthFromFont`` — returns ``SUBSTITUTE_NOTDEF_WIDTH`` for substituted ``.notdef`` (PDFBOX-1900), otherwise the program advance, otherwise the Standard 14 AFM advance, otherwise ``0.0``.

## Wave 71 — Filter parity (ASCII85 alias, deflate-level configuration)

- `pypdfbox/filter/ascii85_filter.py`: new upstream-named alias module mirroring `org.apache.pdfbox.filter.ASCII85Filter`, registered alongside the existing `ASCII85Decode` codec.
- `pypdfbox/filter/filter.py`: added upstream-parity statics `SYSPROP_DEFLATELEVEL`, `SYSPROP_CCITTFAX_MAXBYTES`, and `get_compression_level()`. Java reads these as system properties (`-D...`); the Python port reads them from `os.environ` because Python lacks an equivalent property facility.
- `pypdfbox/filter/flate_decode.py`: `encode` now honours `Filter.get_compression_level()`, matching upstream's `Deflater(getCompressionLevel())` behaviour. Default (`-1`, zlib default) preserves prior output.

## PDType1Font — name-resolution / glyph-probe round-out

- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/font/pd_type1_font.py`: added `ALT_NAMES` ligature-fallback table and `PFB_START_MARKER` constants, `code_to_name(code)`, `get_name_in_font(name)`, `has_glyph(name)`, `has_glyph_for_code(code)`, `read_code(stream | bytes)`, and `get_type1_font()` (alias of `get_font_program`). **Behavior deviation**: upstream `getNameInFont` always has a generic substitute font in hand, so it can return `".notdef"` when neither the embedded program nor the substitute carries the name. The Python port has no built-in system-font substitution path; when there is no embedded program at all, `get_name_in_font` returns the input name unchanged rather than `".notdef"` — we have no negative evidence to remap on. Once an embedded program (or an injected `set_font_program`) is available, behavior matches upstream.

## Action cluster — set_destination validation, URI UTF-8 fallback, JS string ctor

- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/action/pd_action_go_to.py`: `set_destination(PDPageDestination)` now mirrors upstream `PDActionGoTo#setDestination` validation — raises `ValueError` (Python equivalent of `IllegalArgumentException`) when the supplied page-destination array's first element is not a `COSDictionary`. Two pre-existing tests that built local-GoTo destinations via `set_page_number` (a remote-only setter) were updated to call `set_page(<page dict>)` instead.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/action/pd_action_uri.py`: `get_uri()` now matches upstream `PDActionURI.getURI()` byte handling — UTF-16 BE/LE when a BOM is present; otherwise UTF-8 (was previously decoded via `COSDictionary.get_string`, which falls back to PDFDocEncoding and produced mojibake for valid UTF-8 URIs).
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/action/pd_action_java_script.py`: added the upstream string-constructor overload — `PDActionJavaScript("...")` constructs the action and immediately writes the source to `/JS`, matching `PDActionJavaScript(String js)` upstream.

## PDFMergerUtility — stream-cache / compress-parameters parity round-out

- `pypdfbox/multipdf/pdf_merger_utility.py`: added `get_stream_cache_create_function` / `set_stream_cache_create_function` and `get_compress_parameters` / `set_compress_parameters` accessor pairs, mirroring upstream's `mergeDocuments(StreamCacheCreateFunction)` and `mergeDocuments(StreamCacheCreateFunction, CompressParameters)` overloads. `merge_documents` and `merge_documents_random_access_read` now accept `stream_cache_create_function` / `compress_parameters` keyword arguments and stage them on the instance for the duration of the run. Both arguments are advisory only — destination ``PDDocument`` construction in this port does not yet thread them through, matching the existing `memory_usage_setting` parity placeholder.

## Outline cluster — equality / hashing, /Type normalization, iterator cycle-aware hasNext

- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/documentnavigation/outline/pd_outline_node.py`: added `__eq__` / `__hash__` mirroring upstream `PDDictionaryWrapper#equals` / `#hashCode` — two outline wrappers compare equal when (and only when) they wrap the same `COSDictionary` instance, so fresh wrappers returned by `get_next_sibling` / `get_first_child` compare equal to the original. `PDOutlineItemIterator.has_next` is now cycle-aware (matches upstream `PDOutlineItemIterator#hasNext`): returns `False` once the cursor would revisit a previously yielded node, instead of returning `True` followed by a synthetic `StopIteration` from `__next__`.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/documentnavigation/outline/pd_document_outline.py`: constructor now unconditionally writes `/Type /Outlines` on the wrapped dictionary (previously skipped when `/Type` was already present). Mirrors upstream `PDDocumentOutline(COSDictionary)` exactly, normalising stale or wrong `/Type` markers on wrap.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/documentnavigation/outline/pd_outline_item.py`: added `set_previous_sibling` public alias for the internal `_set_previous_sibling` setter, paired with the existing public `set_next_sibling` for hand-built test fixtures.

## Text operator round-out — MoveText null text-line-matrix guard

- `pypdfbox/contentstream/operator/text/move_text.py`: added the upstream `LOG.warn("TextLineMatrix is null, ...")` guard from `org.apache.pdfbox.contentstream.operator.text.MoveText`. Because cluster #2's base `PDFStreamEngine.get_text_line_matrix` returns `None` unconditionally (no real text-state tracking yet), the guard is gated on the engine subclass having actually overridden `get_text_line_matrix` — base-engine callers see no behavior change, rendering / text-extraction subclasses pick the guard up automatically. Mirrors upstream behavior without breaking cluster #2 callers that drive Td without a real text-state object yet.

## Text operator round-out — TD single-leading notification, T* uses COSFloat.ZERO

- `pypdfbox/contentstream/operator/text/move_text_set_leading.py`: ``TD`` now fires the leading change exactly once. Previously the handler invoked `ctx.set_text_leading(...)` directly *and* dispatched a synthetic ``TL`` op when a ``TL`` handler was registered — yielding a double notification (the registered ``SetTextLeading`` handler also calls `ctx.set_text_leading`). The handler now uses the synthetic ``TL`` dispatch when a ``TL`` handler is registered (mirroring upstream's `processOperator(SET_TEXT_LEADING, ...)`) and falls back to a direct engine notification only when no ``TL`` handler exists, so cluster #2 subclasses without a registered ``TL`` still observe the change.
- `pypdfbox/contentstream/operator/text/next_line_op.py`: ``T*`` synthesizes its inner ``Td`` using the interned `COSFloat.ZERO` constant for the ``tx`` operand instead of constructing a fresh `COSFloat(0.0)`, mirroring upstream `org.apache.pdfbox.contentstream.operator.text.NextLine`'s use of `COSFloat.ZERO`.

## Viewer-prefs / page-labels round-out — None-clearing setters and convenience accessors

- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/pd_viewer_preferences.py`: name-valued setters (`set_non_full_screen_page_mode`, `set_reading_direction` / `set_direction`, `set_view_area`, `set_view_clip`, `set_print_area`, `set_print_clip`, `set_duplex`, `set_print_scaling`) now treat `None` as a clear (`remove_item`) instead of writing the literal `/None` token. Java upstream's enum-typed setters can't accept null, so the previous `str(None)` path was a latent bug; pattern matches `PDPageLabelRange.set_style(None)`. Added `get_enforce_names()` / `set_enforce_names(list[str] | None)` typed-list helpers around the existing raw `/Enforce` accessors (PDF 32000-2 §12.4.4) — Python convenience layer; not in upstream PDFBox 3.0.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/pd_page_labels.py`: added `has_label_range(start_page) -> bool`, `remove_label_range(start_page) -> bool`, `get_first_page_index() -> int | None`, and `get_last_page_index() -> int | None`. The first / last accessors mirror Java's `NavigableSet.first()` / `last()` on the result of `getPageIndices()`; the has/remove pair fills in the obvious counterparts to upstream's `setLabelItem`. None of these are in upstream PDFBox 3.0 — Python convenience layer.

## tools/decrypt + tools/encrypt parity round-out

- `pypdfbox/tools/decrypt.py`: now matches upstream `org.apache.pdfbox.tools.Decrypt#call` exit codes — returns 1 (with `Error: Document is not encrypted.`) when the input has no `/Encrypt`, and returns 1 (with `Error: You are only allowed to decrypt a document with the owner password.`) when the supplied password unlocks the document but only with user-level permissions. IO errors are formatted as `Error decrypting document [<class>]: <msg>` to match upstream's stderr line. Adds `-keyStore`/`--keyStore` and `-alias`/`--alias` CLI flags for PKCS#12-keystore-based public-key recipients (CLI surface parity); the end-to-end public-key decryption pipeline is not yet wired into `PDDocument.decrypt`, so passing `-keyStore` surfaces a parity-shaped `Error decrypting document [NotImplementedError]: …` message and exits 4. The library helper `decrypt_pdf` keeps the previous tolerant pass-through (no error gates) — the gates live on the CLI, mirroring upstream's split between `Decrypt#call` and `Loader.loadPDF`.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/encryption/standard_security_handler.py`: `prepare_for_decryption` now sets the `current_access_permission` after a successful key derivation — owner-path matches set `AccessPermission.get_owner_access_permission()`, user-path matches set `AccessPermission(perms).set_read_only()`. R5/R6 default to /P-derived permissions (an owner-vs-user discriminator there is left as future work — none of the existing tests rely on it). Mirrors upstream `StandardSecurityHandler#prepareForDecryption` lines 245-272.
- `pypdfbox/loader.py` + `pypdfbox/pdmodel/pd_document.py`: `Loader.load_pdf`'s auto-decrypt path now stashes the prepared `_security_handler` and `_encryption` on the returned `COSDocument`; `PDDocument.__init__` picks them up so a caller-visible wrapper (built via `PDDocument.load`, which re-wraps the loader's result) reports `get_current_access_permission()` correctly without re-running PBKDF2. Previously the handler attached to a transient decrypt-time `PDDocument` was discarded.
- `pypdfbox/tools/encrypt.py`: `-canPrintFaithful` now drives `AccessPermission.set_can_print_faithful` (the canonical name for the high-quality print bit) instead of the legacy `set_can_print_degraded` alias. Behavior identical (both target the same FAITHFUL_PRINT_BIT); only the call-site name changes.

## PDFunction parity round-out (Type 2 / Type 3 + base aliases)

- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/common/function/pd_function_type2.py`: `eval` now sizes the result by `min(len(/C0), len(/C1))` to match upstream `PDFunctionType2.eval` (`new float[Math.min(c0.size(), c1.size())]`). Previously the port sized by `len(/C0)` and padded a short `/C1` with zeros — a defensive divergence that had no actual call-site dependency. Restoring upstream parity makes shading/tint output dimensions match Java byte-for-byte. Added `__str__` matching upstream `toString()` format `"FunctionType2{C0: <c0> C1: <c1> N: <n>}"` (the base class still falls through to `"FunctionType<n>"` for Types 0, 3, 4 — upstream only overrides on Type 2).
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/common/function/pd_function_type3.py`: added `get_functions_array() -> COSArray | None` (raw-array accessor mirroring upstream `getFunctions()` which returns `COSArray`; the existing `get_functions()` continues to return a materialised `list[PDFunction]` for the typical eval path) and `get_encode_for_parameter(n) -> tuple[float, float] | None` (publicly exposed counterpart to upstream's private `getEncodeForParameter(int)`).
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/common/function/pd_function.py`: added `get_domain_values()` / `get_range_values()` / `set_domain_values()` / `set_range_values()` upstream-named aliases (PDFBox `getDomainValues` is private and `setDomainValues`/`setRangeValues` are public; we expose all four for symmetry).

## graphics operator parity (DrawObject / ShadingFill validation + upstream-name aliases)

- `pypdfbox/contentstream/operator/graphics/shading_fill.py`: `process` now raises `MissingOperandException` when called with an empty operand list, matching upstream `ShadingFill.process` (`if (operands.isEmpty()) throw new MissingOperandException(...)`). When operand 0 is not a `COSName` the operator silently returns rather than upstream's second `MissingOperandException` throw — pypdfbox follows the leniency precedent established by the path operators (`MoveTo`, `LineTo`, `CurveTo`, `AppendRectangleToPath`) which silently skip type-mismatched operands in malformed content streams.
- `pypdfbox/contentstream/operator/graphics/invoke_named_xobject.py`: `process` now raises `MissingOperandException` on an empty operand list (upstream `DrawObject.process` first guard) and silently returns when operand 0 is not a `COSName` (upstream `if (!(base0 instanceof COSName)) return;` early-return). The existing test that asserted the lite-stub no-op behaviour ("engine layer will validate") was updated to assert the new upstream-parity behaviour.
- `pypdfbox/contentstream/operator/graphics/__init__.py`: added upstream-name aliases re-binding the existing concrete handlers — `DrawObject = InvokeNamedXObject`, `FillNonZeroRule = FillPathNonZeroWinding`, `FillEvenOddRule = FillPathEvenOdd`, `ClipNonZeroRule = ClipNonZeroWinding`, `ClipEvenOddRule = ClipEvenOdd`, `EndPath = EndPathNoOp`. Code ported from upstream PDFBox can keep its original imports (`from pypdfbox.contentstream.operator.graphics import FillNonZeroRule`); `DrawObject is InvokeNamedXObject` etc. so registry lookups and `isinstance` checks continue to work unchanged. `StrokePath` and `ClosePath` are also re-exported from the graphics package (they already use their upstream names in `path/`) so all upstream `org.apache.pdfbox.contentstream.operator.graphics` class identifiers resolve via this package.

## PDFileSpecification family parity round-out

- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/common/filespecification/pd_file_specification.py`: `create_fs` now dereferences a `COSObject` indirect reference before classification — when given an unresolved indirect ref it returns `None` (matches the `null` outcome upstream gets after parser dereferencing); when resolved to a `COSString` or `COSDictionary` it dispatches as if called with the dereferenced value. Upstream Java relies on the parser having already auto-dereffed by the time `createFS(COSBase)` is called, so this is a small behavioural extension to avoid surprising `OSError("Unknown file specification …")` in pypdfbox callers that pass through raw refs.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/common/filespecification/pd_embedded_file.py`: added `get_mac_res_fork()` / `set_mac_res_fork()` mechanical-name aliases delegating to the existing `get_mac_resource_fork()` / `set_mac_resource_fork()` methods. Upstream `getMacResFork` / `setMacResFork` are typed `String`; the type deviation to `COSStream` (PDF spec `/ResFork` is a stream) is the one previously documented under this file's enrichment entry — these aliases cover the upstream method names verbatim.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/common/filespecification/pd_embedded_file.py`: added `get_check_sum_string()` / `set_check_sum_string()` string-form aliases mirroring upstream `getCheckSum()` / `setCheckSum(String)` verbatim (upstream return type is `String`). The byte-form `get_check_sum()` / `set_check_sum()` introduced earlier (with hex-form encoding for non-printable bytes) remains the primary pypdfbox API; the string-form aliases are provided for callers that prefer the upstream-typed surface. Added class-level Type-name constants `PDComplexFileSpecification.FILESPEC` (`"Filespec"`) and `PDEmbeddedFile.EMBEDDED_FILE` (`"EmbeddedFile"`) matching upstream `COSName.FILESPEC` / `COSName.EMBEDDED_FILE`.

## XMP simple-type parity round-out (URLType / RenditionClassType)

- `pypdfbox/xmpbox/type/type_mapping.py`: the `"URL"` short type-name now resolves to the new `URLType` class (was previously aliased to `URIType` while the dedicated class was unported), matching upstream `Types.URL(true, Text, URLType.class)`. `create_url(...)` now returns `URLType`; a new `create_rendition_class(...)` factory mirrors upstream `TypeMapping.createRenditionClass`. The `"RenditionClass"` short type-name is registered for `instanciate_simple_property`.

## Name-tree subclass parity round-out

- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/documentnavigation/destination/pd_destination_name_tree_node.py`: `convert_cos_to_value` now mirrors upstream `convertCOSToPD` — when the leaf COS value is a `COSDictionary`, the `/D` entry is dereferenced before dispatch (named-destination indirection inlined into the leaf). Invalid leaf entries return `None` instead of raising, matching PDFBOX-5975. Previous pypdfbox behaviour raised `OSError` on any non-`PDPageDestination` result.

## XObject factory parity (PDXObject.create_x_object)

- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/graphics/pd_x_object.py`: added `PDXObject.create_x_object(base, resources)` static factory mirroring upstream `PDXObject.createXObject(COSBase, PDResources)`. Dispatches `/Subtype /Image` → `PDImageXObject`, `/Subtype /Form` → `PDFormXObject` (the `PDTransparencyGroup` form subclass is not yet ported — the plain `PDFormXObject` is returned, with the group dictionary still discoverable via `get_group_attributes()`). `/Subtype /PS` raises `OSError("PDPostScriptXObject is not yet supported")` instead of returning a `PDPostScriptXObject` instance because that subclass is not yet ported. Other subtype values raise `OSError` matching upstream's `IOException("Invalid XObject Subtype: …")`. Non-stream `base` raises `OSError("Unexpected object type: …")`. **Closed (Wave 1305 audit):** `PDPostScriptXObject` (`pypdfbox/pdmodel/graphics/pd_post_script_x_object.py`) and `PDTransparencyGroup` (`pypdfbox/pdmodel/graphics/form/pd_transparency_group.py`, extends `PDFormXObject`) are both ported. The factory dispatch can now return the typed `PDPostScriptXObject` for `/Subtype /PS` and a `PDTransparencyGroup` for form XObjects carrying `/Group /S /Transparency`.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/graphics/form/pd_form_x_object.py`: added typed `get_group_attributes() / set_group_attributes(PDTransparencyGroupAttributes)` mirroring upstream `getGroup() / setGroup(PDTransparencyGroupAttributes)`. The pre-existing raw `get_group() / set_group(COSDictionary)` accessors are retained for backward compatibility with call sites that already store the dictionary directly. `set_group(...)` invalidates the typed cache so a subsequent `get_group_attributes()` re-wraps the freshly installed dict.

## tools/split + tools/merge CLI parity round-out

- `pypdfbox/tools/split.py`: added `-password` / `--password` flag (passes through to `PDDocument.load(..., password=...)`) — matches upstream `PDFSplit.java`'s `@Option(names = "-password", interactive = true)`. Page-range default behaviour now matches upstream: when `-startPage` and/or `-endPage` are supplied without an explicit `-split`, the entire range is emitted as a SINGLE output file (upstream calls `setSplitAtPage(numberOfPages)` / `setSplitAtPage(endPage)` in this branch — see `PDFSplit.java#call`). Previously pypdfbox always defaulted `-split` to 1 and produced one file per page in the range — a behavioural deviation from upstream PDFSplit. The `-split` argument now uses `-1` as the upstream "not supplied" sentinel; an explicit `0` or negative value still exits 2 with the existing `-split must be >= 1` message.
- `pypdfbox/tools/merge.py`: load / save errors now emit upstream `PDFMerger.java`'s user-visible error format `"Error merging documents [<ExcClass>]: <message>"` and exit 4, instead of bubbling the `OSError` up to the cli-level catch-all. The minimum-two-inputs guard (a deliberate pypdfbox UX deviation; upstream picocli accepts arity >= 1) is retained.

## SetStrokingColorN / SetNonStrokingColorN / SetStrokingColorSpace / SetNonStrokingColorSpace parity round-out

- `pypdfbox/contentstream/operator/color/set_stroking_color_n.py`, `set_non_stroking_color_n.py`: lifted from registry-only stubs to full upstream parity. `SCN` / `scn` now subclass `SetStrokingColor` / `SetNonStrokingColor` (matching the upstream hierarchy) and override `process` to also handle `PDPattern` colour spaces -- for non-Pattern spaces the operand-count and `COSNumber`-type checks mirror upstream `SetColor.process`, including the PDFBOX-5851 invalid-`PDColor([], None)` fallback on type mismatch. The pre-existing leniency deviation from upstream -- `SetColor.process` returns silently on too-few operands instead of upstream raising `MissingOperandException` -- is inherited from the parent `SetStrokingColor` / `SetNonStrokingColor` operator family.
- `pypdfbox/contentstream/operator/color/set_stroking_color_space.py`, `set_non_stroking_color_space.py`: lifted from registry-only stubs. `CS` / `cs` now resolve the operand `COSName` through `PDFStreamEngine.get_resources().get_color_space(name)`, install the resolved `PDColorSpace` on the graphics state via `set_stroking_color_space` / `set_non_stroking_color_space`, and reset the corresponding stroking / non-stroking colour to the new colour space's `get_initial_color()` (matching upstream). Honours `is_should_process_color_operators()` for Type3 charprocs / uncoloured tiling patterns. Standalone (no engine) and malformed operand lists remain tolerant no-ops, matching the existing leniency precedent for content-stream operators in pypdfbox.

## DateConverter port — xmpbox utility for ISO 8601 / PDF date strings

- `pypdfbox/xmpbox/date_converter.py` (NEW): port of `org.apache.xmpbox.DateConverter`. Surfaces `DateConverter.to_calendar(str | None) -> datetime | None` and `DateConverter.to_iso8601(datetime, print_millis=False) -> str` (also exposed as module-level functions). Returns timezone-aware `datetime` instead of `java.util.Calendar`; naive ISO 8601 strings are anchored to UTC (matches upstream `fromISO8601` `LocalDateTime.atZone(ZoneId.of("UTC"))` fallback). Year 0 input is rejected with `OSError` because Python `datetime` does not support year 0 — upstream `0000-01-01` round-trips to `0001-01-01` via `GregorianCalendar` BCE handling. The constructor raises `TypeError` to mirror upstream `private DateConverter()`. Wired into `pypdfbox.xmpbox.__init__` for re-export.

## ContentStreamWriter — write_tokens iterable acceptance

- `pypdfbox/pdfwriter/content_stream_writer.py`: `write_tokens(...)` now accepts any iterable (generator, `deque`, `map`, custom `Iterable`) as the single-argument `List<?>` overload, not just `list` / `tuple`. Token / bytes-like single-argument calls (`write_tokens(cos_array)`, `write_tokens(b"raw")`) keep their varargs semantics. Upstream's Java signature is `writeTokens(List<?>)` only; this is a Pythonic ergonomic extension — output bytes are unchanged for any input upstream would have accepted.

## PDFontDescriptor — mask-based flag accessors + set_panose writer

- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/font/pd_font_descriptor.py`: round-out additions. New public `is_flag_bit_on(mask)` / `set_flag_bit(mask, value)` mask-based flag accessors mirror upstream's *private* `isFlagBitOn(int)` / `setFlagBit(int, boolean)` helpers — exposed publicly here because the `FLAG_*` constants are part of the pypdfbox public surface; complements the existing `get_flag(bit)` / `set_flag(bit, value)` 1-based-index accessors. New `set_panose(PDPanose | bytes | bytearray | None)` writer is a pypdfbox extension — upstream PDFBox 3.0 `PDFontDescriptor` exposes only the read side (`getPanose()`); the new writer lazily creates the `/Style` dict, stores the 12-byte buffer as a `COSString`, and on `None` removes `/Panose` (and the now-empty `/Style` dict). Accepts a `PDPanose` wrapper or raw 12-byte buffer. No upstream method renamed or behaviour changed.

## PDDocumentCatalog — get_acro_form fixup overload + auto-create /AA, /Threads parity

- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/pd_document_catalog.py`: `get_acro_form()` now accepts an optional `acro_form_fixup` argument (mirrors upstream `getAcroForm(PDDocumentFixup)` overload). When supplied, the fixup's `apply()` is invoked once, the cached wrapper is invalidated, and the same fixup instance on subsequent calls is treated as already applied (matches upstream `acroFormFixupApplied` tracking). pypdfbox does not yet ship `AcroFormDefaultFixup`, so the no-arg `get_acro_form()` continues to return the unfixed AcroForm — passing `None` is the documented way to request that behaviour, parity with upstream's `getAcroForm(null)` contract.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/pd_document_catalog.py`: `get_actions()` now auto-creates an empty `/AA` sub-dictionary when absent (mirrors upstream's `if (addAction == null) { addAction = new COSDictionary(); root.setItem(COSName.AA, addAction); }` pattern). Always returns a non-`None` `PDDocumentCatalogAdditionalActions`. Previous behaviour returned `None` when `/AA` was absent.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/pd_document_catalog.py`: `get_threads()` now auto-creates an empty `/Threads` array when absent (mirrors upstream `if (array == null) { array = new COSArray(); ... root.setItem(COSName.THREADS, array); }`). Returned list is always empty when no threads are present, but the catalog now records `/Threads` as a side effect — matches upstream so callers that mutate the returned list-like value write back into the catalog. Java's `array.setDirect(false)` flag has no Python analogue and is omitted.

## PDSignature.__str__ + PDSignatureField parity round-out

- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/digitalsignature/pd_signature.py`: new `__str__` returns a compact `PDSignature(name=..., reason=..., location=..., date=..., contact=...)` summary of the populated identity fields. Java's `Object.toString()` is `ClassName@hashcode` which is not useful for end users; upstream `PDSignatureField.getValueAsString()` calls `signature.toString()` so the lite port routes that through this populated-fields summary instead of the bare repr. Empty/absent entries are omitted; when nothing is populated the body is the placeholder `<empty>`.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/form/pd_signature_field.py`: `get_value_as_string()` now returns `str(self.get_signature())` when `/V` is present (was: always `""`). Mirrors upstream `PDSignatureField.getValueAsString()` which delegates to `signature.toString()`. Empty when no signature is set, matching upstream's null-check.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/form/pd_signature_field.py`: `set_value(PDSignature)` now invokes `self.apply_change()` after writing `/V`. Mirrors upstream `PDSignatureField.setValue(PDSignature)` which ends with `applyChange()` so subclasses overriding `apply_change` for cache invalidation get notified. The lite port's default `apply_change` is still a logging no-op (appearance regeneration deferred).

## ArrayProperty — complex-property surface round-out

- `pypdfbox/xmpbox/type/array_property.py`: added `get_property(field_name)`, `get_array_property(field_name)`, `get_properties_by_local_name(local_name)`, plus `add_namespace` / `get_namespace_prefix` / `get_all_namespaces_with_prefix` namespace-map accessors. Mirrors the `AbstractComplexProperty` surface that upstream's `ArrayProperty` inherits — the pypdfbox port skipped `AbstractComplexProperty` so these were missing on `ArrayProperty` directly. **Behavior deviation**: `get_array_property(field_name)` returns `None` when the named child exists but is not an `ArrayProperty`; upstream casts unconditionally and would raise `ClassCastException`. Returning `None` keeps the call site type-safe and matches the existing `get_array_property` shape on `AbstractStructuredType` in the pypdfbox port.

## FontMapper / FontInfo / FontProvider / CIDFontMapping — interface round-out

- `pypdfbox/fontbox/font_format.py` + `font_info.py` + `font_provider.py` + `cid_font_mapping.py` (NEW): port the missing pieces of the upstream FontMapper SPI. `FontFormat` is a plain enum (TTF/OTF/PFB). `FontInfo` is the abstract per-font descriptor returned by `FontProvider.get_font_info()`; the package-private upstream helpers `getWeightClassAsPanose` / `getCodePageRange` are surfaced as public methods (`get_weight_class_as_panose` / `get_code_page_range`) since Python has no equivalent visibility. `FontProvider` is the abstract enumeration source — pypdfbox does **not** ship a concrete `FileSystemFontProvider` (upstream's default scans system font directories via java.awt; bringing that to Python would require a new dep like fontconfig / matplotlib.font_manager). Apps wanting full system-font enumeration plug their own provider into a custom `FontMapper`.
- `pypdfbox/fontbox/font_mapper.py`: added the third interface method `get_cid_font(base_font, font_descriptor, cid_system_info) -> CIDFontMapping | None`. **Deviation**: upstream Java declares this `abstract` on the interface; pypdfbox keeps it concrete with a default `None` return so existing `FontMapper` subclasses (and the pypdfbox `DefaultFontMapper`, which has no system-font scanner) don't spontaneously become abstract. Real CID-aware mappers override it to return a `CIDFontMapping`.

## Overlay — `_load_pdfs` filename precedence parity

- `pypdfbox/multipdf/overlay.py`: `_load_pdfs` now mirrors upstream's load-order: when **both** a filename and a `PDDocument` are configured for the same overlay slot, the filename wins — the document is reloaded from disk and any previously-staged `PDDocument` is replaced. Previously the pypdfbox port short-circuited (`if filename is not None and document is None`) so a setter-supplied PDF blocked the file reload. Affects `set_input_file` / `set_input_pdf`, plus the parallel default / first / last / odd / even / all-pages overlay slots. Mirrors `Overlay.loadPDFs` in PDFBox 3.0.x.

## PDArtifactMarkedContent — `getNameAsString` parity for `/Type` and `/Subtype`

- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/documentinterchange/taggedpdf/pd_artifact_marked_content.py`: `get_type()` and `get_subtype()` now resolve both `COSName` and `COSString` operands, mirroring upstream `COSDictionary.getNameAsString(COSName)`. The previous lookup went through pypdfbox's stricter `COSDictionary.get_name`, which only resolves `COSName` operands and silently returned `None` for `COSString`-encoded artifact types. Authoring tools occasionally store `/Type` and `/Subtype` as strings; the artifact accessors now match upstream's tolerant read. Added `get_bbox()` alias for `get_b_box()` matching the spelling already used by sibling pypdfbox wrappers (e.g. `PDPage.get_bbox`).

## PDAcroForm — set_fields(None) hardening + raw /SigFlags accessors

- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/form/pd_acro_form.py`: `set_fields()` now accepts `None` and treats it as "clear /Fields to an empty array" (mirrors the constructor's seeded shape). Upstream `setFields(null)` would NPE in `new COSArray(null)`; pypdfbox normalises so callers can reset the form to "no fields" with a single call. The cache is invalidated, matching the existing list-replacement path.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/form/pd_acro_form.py`: new `get_signature_flags()` / `set_signature_flags(int)` raw `/SigFlags` accessors, plus public class-level `FLAG_SIGNATURES_EXIST` / `FLAG_APPEND_ONLY` constants. Upstream keeps the bit constants package-private and exposes only the typed `isSignaturesExist` / `setAppendOnly` bit-by-bit helpers; pypdfbox surfaces the raw integer too so callers can preserve reserved bits across round-trips. The typed setters continue to mask their bit only — unrelated bits round-trip untouched. Also fixed a Python-3 except-clause bug (`except AttributeError, TypeError:` was binding `AttributeError` as the variable named `TypeError`, never catching `TypeError` from numeric coercion in the form-XObject `/BBox` reader); now `except (AttributeError, TypeError):`.

## PDDocumentNameDictionary / PDDocumentNameDestinationDictionary — surface round-out

- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/pd_document_name_dictionary.py`: added `is_empty()` predicate and `__bool__`. Upstream PDFBox does not expose ``isEmpty()`` on the wrapper directly; callers reach through ``getCOSObject().isEmpty()``. pypdfbox surfaces it on the wrapper for symmetry with ``COSDictionary.is_empty()``. Also added `get_java_script()` as the strict snake_case translation of upstream ``getJavaScript()`` — the existing ``get_javascript()`` (lowercase ``script``) form is retained as the original spelling.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/pd_document_name_destination_dictionary.py`: added `is_empty()`, `get_names() -> list[str]`, `__contains__(name)` (string or COSName) and `__len__()`. Upstream ``PDDocumentNameDestinationDictionary`` exposes only ``getDestination(String)`` so callers cannot enumerate or test membership without constructing every destination wrapper; the new accessors delegate to ``COSDictionary.is_empty`` / ``key_set`` / ``contains_key`` / ``size``. ``__contains__`` distinguishes ``key missing`` from ``key present, value malformed`` (the latter still returns ``None`` from ``get_destination``).

## PDFontDescriptor — getNameAsString lenience + value-type dunders

- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/font/pd_font_descriptor.py`: `get_font_name()` and `get_font_stretch()` now resolve both `COSName` and `COSString` operands, mirroring upstream `COSDictionary.getNameAsString(COSName)`. The previous lookup went through pypdfbox's stricter `COSDictionary.get_name`, which only resolves `COSName` and silently returned `None` for `COSString`-encoded font names — a tolerance gap against upstream's `dic.getNameAsString(FONT_NAME)` / `getNameAsString(FONT_STRETCH)` calls. Also added a diagnostic `__repr__` on `PDFontDescriptor` (`PDFontDescriptor(font_name=..., flags=...)`) — pypdfbox extension; upstream inherits Java's default ``Object.toString``. `PDPanose` and `PDPanoseClassification` gained value-equality (`__eq__` / `__hash__`) and `__repr__` based on their underlying byte buffers — pypdfbox extensions; upstream relies on Java's default identity ``equals`` / ``hashCode``. Treating these immutable wrappers as value types lets callers compare and hash PANOSE blocks (e.g. as dict keys, in test assertions).

## PDFontDescriptor — class-level FLAG constants + has_font_file predicates + PDPanose.with_panose_classification

- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/font/pd_font_descriptor.py`: exposed the nine `FLAG_*` masks as public class attributes on `PDFontDescriptor` (`PDFontDescriptor.FLAG_FORCE_BOLD` etc.). Mirrors upstream's `private static final int FLAG_*` declarations on the Java class — Python promotes them to public class attrs so callers can write the dotted form after porting (the module-level constants remain available for back-compat). Added `has_font_file()` / `has_font_file2()` / `has_font_file3()` presence predicates that delegate to `COSDictionary.contains_key` — pypdfbox extensions that avoid materialising a `PDStream` wrapper when callers only need to know which font program slot is populated. `PDPanose.with_panose_classification(PDPanoseClassification | bytes | bytearray)` returns a new `PDPanose` with bytes 2-11 replaced and the leading 2-byte sFamilyClass preserved verbatim — pypdfbox extension; upstream `PDPanose` is read-only. Returns a fresh wrapper to preserve immutable value semantics.

## PDDocumentCatalog — /NeedsRendering + /OCProperties long-form alias + /URI /Base shortcut

- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/pd_document_catalog.py`: new `is_needs_rendering()` / `set_needs_rendering(bool | None)` accessors for `/NeedsRendering` (PDF 32000-1 §7.7.3.4 Table 28; PDF 1.7+). Defaults to `False` when the entry is absent, mirroring the spec default. Upstream PDFBox 3.0 does not surface this flag on `PDDocumentCatalog`; pypdfbox ports it for forward parity with the 4.x line. `None` removes the entry so callers can return to the implicit default. Added `get_optional_content_properties()` / `set_optional_content_properties()` long-form aliases for `get_oc_properties()` / `set_oc_properties()` matching the descriptive PDF 32000-1 §8.11.4 dictionary name; the alias shares the version-bump side effect (auto-bump to 1.5 when non-`None`). Added `get_base_uri()` / `set_base_uri(str | None)` catalog-level shortcuts that read/write `/URI /Base` without forcing callers to materialise `PDURIDictionary` for the common single-string case (mirrors the `is_document_marked` / `has_user_properties` MarkInfo shortcut style). `set_base_uri` materialises `/URI` on demand and removes the empty `/URI` dictionary when `set_base_uri(None)` clears the last entry; sibling `/URI` keys are preserved when present.

## PDAcroForm — predicate helpers + QUADDING constants

- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/form/pd_acro_form.py`: added `has_fields()` / `is_empty()` / `has_calc_order()` predicate helpers and class-level `QUADDING_LEFT` / `QUADDING_CENTERED` / `QUADDING_RIGHT` constants. Upstream PDFBox does not surface these directly — callers reach through `getFields().isEmpty()` / `getCalcOrder().isEmpty()` / `PDVariableText.QUADDING_*`; the new predicates avoid materialising the full field-list (and resolving every entry through `PDFieldFactory`) when callers only need a presence check, and the QUADDING constants let `set_q` callers stay within the `PDAcroForm` namespace. `is_empty()` combines `has_fields()` with `has_xfa()` — an "empty" AcroForm is one whose catalog entry could safely be dropped without losing user data (no widget references and no XFA datasets).

## DateType — DateConverter delegation for string parsing + serialization

- `pypdfbox/xmpbox/type/date_type.py`: `set_value(str)` now delegates to `DateConverter.toCalendar` (Python `to_calendar`) and `get_string_value()` delegates to `DateConverter.toISO8601` (`to_iso8601`), matching upstream `org.apache.xmpbox.type.DateType` which calls `DateConverter.toCalendar` from `setValueFromString` and `DateConverter.toISO8601` from `getStringValue`. The earlier port routed through stdlib `datetime.fromisoformat` / `isoformat` which rejected partial ISO 8601 forms (`YYYY`, `YYYY-MM`) and PDF dictionary form (`D:YYYYMMDDhhmmss`) that upstream accepts; the delegation closes that gap. The earlier-noted "ISO 8601 round-trip via datetime.isoformat / fromisoformat instead of upstream's DateConverter" deviation is therefore retracted.

## PDFRenderer — ImageType + RenderDestination enums + accessor parity

- `pypdfbox/rendering/image_type.py` (NEW): port of upstream `org.apache.pdfbox.rendering.ImageType` — `BINARY` / `GRAY` / `RGB` / `ARGB` / `BGR` enum members each carrying a `to_buffered_image_type()` returning the matching `java.awt.image.BufferedImage.TYPE_*` int constant (1, 2, 5, 10, 12). Added Python-side `pil_mode` helper that returns the Pillow `Image.new(mode=...)` value; BGR collapses to "RGB" (Pillow has no packed-BGR mode).
- `pypdfbox/rendering/render_destination.py` (NEW): port of upstream `org.apache.pdfbox.rendering.RenderDestination` — `EXPORT` / `VIEW` / `PRINT` enum. Values are the title-case strings already consumed by `PDOptionalContentProperties.get_render_state`.
- `pypdfbox/rendering/pdf_renderer.py`: added 6 upstream-named accessors — `get_annotations_filter()` / `set_annotations_filter(callable)` (default accepts every annotation, `lambda a: True`); `get_rendering_hints()` / `set_rendering_hints(hints)` (store-only — Java2D hints have no Pillow equivalent); `get_page_image()` returns the most recently rendered image, mirroring upstream's package-private accessor (None before first render); `get_document()` returns the document being rendered (upstream's `protected final document` field exposed as a getter); `is_group_enabled(PDOptionalContentGroup)` consults catalog `/OCProperties` (returns True when no /OCProperties present, otherwise delegates to `is_group_enabled`). Added `image_type: ImageType | None` overload parameter to `render_image` and `render_image_with_dpi` mirroring upstream's three-arg `renderImageWithDPI(int, float, ImageType)` overload — the lite renderer rasterises onto an RGB canvas (aggdraw's only supported mode) and converts the result via `Image.convert` at teardown when a non-RGB target is requested. ARGB conversion tags pure-white pixels as fully transparent so the alpha matches what upstream's transparent-canvas render produces. `set_default_destination` now also accepts a `RenderDestination` enum value (still stored as the bare string for back-compat with existing string-based getters).

## CompressParameters — value-type semantics + functional update + disabled predicate

- `pypdfbox/pdfwriter/compress/compress_parameters.py`: added `__eq__` / `__hash__` / `__repr__` so two instances configured with the same `object_stream_size` compare equal and hash to the same bucket — pypdfbox extension; upstream relies on Java's default identity equality. Added `with_object_stream_size(int)` functional update helper that returns a new instance with the requested size (or `self` when unchanged) — convenience over re-instantiating since the value type is conceptually immutable (upstream's field is `final`). Added `is_disabled()` predicate (inverse of `is_compress`) for symmetry with the predicate-pair pattern used elsewhere in the writer cluster (`COSWriterXRefEntry.is_free` / `with_free`). `__init__` now rejects non-int `object_stream_size` with a `TypeError` rather than silently `int()`-coercing strings/floats.

## PDTransparencyGroup — form X-object subclass port + factory dispatch

- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/graphics/form/pd_transparency_group.py` (NEW): port of upstream `org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.graphics.form.PDTransparencyGroup`. Subclass of `PDFormXObject` carrying no additional state — constructed directly when a `/Subtype /Form` stream advertises a `/Group` dict with `/S /Transparency` (PDF 32000-1 §11.6.6). Constructor accepts the same overload surface as `PDFormXObject` (`PDStream` / `COSStream` / `PDDocument` plus optional `PDResourceCache`).
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/graphics/pd_x_object.py`: `PDXObject.create_x_object` now dispatches `/Subtype /Form` streams whose `/Group /S` is `/Transparency` to `PDTransparencyGroup`; other `/Subtype /Form` streams (no `/Group`, `/Group` not a dict, `/Group` without `/S`, `/Group` with non-Transparency `/S`) still fall back to plain `PDFormXObject`. Closes the deferred-subclass note in the previous PDXObject factory entry.

## PDCIDFont default position vector — upstream-correct (width/2, dw2[0])

- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/font/pd_cid_font.py`: `get_position_vector(cid)` now returns upstream's `(widthForCID(cid)/2, dw2[0])` formula when `/W2` has no entry for the CID. Previous wave used `(dw2[1], dw2[0])` (effectively `(-1000, 880)` for the spec defaults), which mismatched upstream `PDCIDFont.getDefaultPositionVector`. With `/DW = 1000` and the spec's `/DW2 = [880 -1000]` defaults the descendant default is now `(500, 880)` — and the parent `PDType0Font.get_position_vector` scales it to `(-0.5, -0.88)` rather than `(1.0, -0.88)`. CIDs explicitly listed in `/W2` are unaffected.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/font/pd_type0_font.py`: surfaced upstream-named CMap-classification predicates `is_cmap_predefined()` (true when `/Encoding` is a `COSName`) and `is_descendant_cjk()` (true for descendant `/CIDSystemInfo` registry `Adobe` and ordering in `GB1`/`CNS1`/`Japan1`/`Korea1`). Added `__repr__` matching upstream's `toString` format (`PDType0Font/<DescendantClass>, PostScript name: <BaseFont>`). Module-level constants `IDENTITY_H = "Identity-H"` and `IDENTITY_V = "Identity-V"` exposed for callers building Type 0 dictionaries (counterparts of upstream's `COSName.IDENTITY_H` / `IDENTITY_V`).

## PDPage / PDPageTree — rotation gate, annotation_filter, get_count parity

- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/pd_page.py`: `get_rotation()` now matches upstream's `% 90 == 0` gate exactly — non-multiples of 90 (e.g. 45, 89, 271) return 0 instead of being snapped to the nearest 90. Negative multiples of 90 still wrap via `(angle % 360 + 360) % 360`. Closes a behavioural divergence from upstream `PDPage.getRotation()`.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/pd_page.py`: `get_annotations(annotation_filter=None)` now accepts an optional `Callable[[PDAnnotation], bool]` mirroring upstream's two-overload pair `getAnnotations()` / `getAnnotations(AnnotationFilter)`. `None` (default) accepts every annotation. Also added defensive skip for null `/Annots` array entries (matches upstream's `if (item == null) continue;`).
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/pd_page_tree.py`: `get_count()` now reads `/Count` directly via `root.getInt(COUNT, 0)` mirroring upstream literally — O(1), and returns whatever `/Count` says even when it disagrees with a tree walk. Use `len(self)` (the Python protocol) for a walk-validated count. `pypdfbox/multipdf/splitter.py` updated to use `len(page_tree)` so its iteration still walks even when `/Count` is wrong.

## PDListBox + PDSignatureField — visibility-aware construct_appearances + own-dictionary predicates

- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/form/pd_signature_field.py`: `construct_appearances()` now mirrors upstream's `PDSignatureField.constructAppearances` visibility check — when the first widget has a non-zero rectangle and is neither hidden nor no-view, the lite port emits the same "appearance generation for signature fields not implemented here … see CreateVisibleSignature*.java" warning via `logging` (upstream uses `commons-logging`). Earlier waves left this branch as a bare no-op, which silently dropped the upstream warning. Hidden / no-view / zero-rect / no-rect / no-widgets cases continue to short-circuit to a true no-op, also matching upstream.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/form/pd_signature_field.py`: pypdfbox-only own-dictionary predicates `has_signature()`, `has_default_value()`, `has_seed_value()`, `has_lock()` (each `contains_key` on the underlying field dict). Cheaper than `get_*() is not None` since they skip the `PDSignature` / `PDSeedValue` / `PDSignatureLock` wrapper construction; mirror the predicate-pair pattern already established for `PDTextField.has_value` / `has_default_value`.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/form/pd_list_box.py`: pypdfbox-only own-dictionary predicate `has_top_index()` (`contains_key(/TI)`). Lets callers distinguish "explicit ``/TI = 0``" from "no ``/TI`` entry" — `get_top_index()` returns `0` for both cases, matching upstream's `getInt(TI, 0)` default.

## PDType3CharProc — charproc-local /Resources (PDFBOX-5294) + get_bbox upstream-aligned + d0/d1 predicates

- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/font/pd_type3_char_proc.py`: `get_resources()` now honours a `/Resources` entry stashed on the char-proc stream itself, matching upstream `PDType3CharProc.getResources()` / PDFBOX-5294 ("Using resources dictionary found in charproc entry"). Previous implementation unconditionally fell back to the parent font's `/Resources`, which meant a malformed PDF that placed `/Resources` on the char-proc would drop those resources during processing. Returns a fresh `PDResources` over the local dict when present; otherwise falls back to `font.get_resources()`.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/font/pd_type3_char_proc.py`: `get_bbox()` now matches upstream `getBBox()` exactly — returns `font.get_font_bbox()` directly. Previous version preferred a `d1`-derived per-glyph bbox over the font's `/FontBBox`, which was a behavioural deviation from the `PDContentStream.getBBox()` contract. Per-glyph bounds remain available via `get_glyph_bbox()` (which already mirrored upstream `getGlyphBBox()`). When the font has no `/FontBBox` we still return an empty `PDRectangle` rather than `None` because the `PDContentStream.get_bbox()` return type is non-`Optional`.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/font/pd_type3_char_proc.py`: pypdfbox-only predicates `has_d0()`, `has_d1()`, `has_resources()` for cheap leading-operator and local-resources detection without re-tokenising or constructing a `PDResources` wrapper.

## PDActionResetForm — FLAG_INCLUDE_EXCLUDE constant + extended set_fields + get_field_names

- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/action/pd_action_reset_form.py`: exposed `FLAG_INCLUDE_EXCLUDE` as a public class constant (was module-private `_FLAG_INCLUDE_EXCLUDE`) so callers manipulating raw `/Flags` integers can address bit 1 (PDF 32000-1 §12.7.5.3 Table 239) symbolically. Extended `set_fields(values)` to also accept a list of `PDField` instances or a list of raw `COSBase` entries (each stored as-is — the spec lets `/Fields` entries be partial names, fully qualified names, or indirect references) in addition to the existing `COSArray | None` overloads, mirroring the convenience surface added to `PDActionSubmitForm.set_fields` in Wave 26. Non-`PDField`/non-`COSBase` list entries raise `TypeError` rather than coercing silently. New `get_field_names()` helper extracts the string-form (`COSString`) names from `/Fields` in array order, skipping dictionary / reference entries — handy for callers that only care about the named subset and don't want to reach through `PDAcroForm` resolution. Returns `[]` when `/Fields` is absent or has no string entries; tolerant of malformed `/Fields` (returns `[]` rather than raising when the entry is present but non-array). Existing `get_fields() -> COSArray | None` signature preserved for upstream parity with `PDActionResetForm.getFields()`.

## PDVariableText — inheritable /Q + /RV stream payload via to_text_string + has_* predicates

- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/form/pd_variable_text.py`: `get_q()` now walks the inheritable-attribute chain (`self -> parent -> AcroForm`) via `get_inheritable_attribute(/Q)`, matching upstream `PDVariableText.getQ()` (`COSNumber number = (COSNumber)getInheritableAttribute(COSName.Q);`). Previous implementation called `self._field.get_int(/Q, 0)` — local-only — which silently masked an inherited justification setting on the AcroForm or a parent non-terminal field. Defaults to `QUADDING_LEFT` (0) when no ancestor sets it; tolerant of `COSFloat` payloads by routing through `COSNumber.int_value()`.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/form/pd_variable_text.py`: `get_rich_text_value()` now decodes a `COSStream`-valued `/RV` via `COSStream.to_text_string()` (which mirrors upstream `COSStream.toTextString` — UTF-16BE BOM / UTF-8 BOM / PDFDocEncoding fallback through the `/Filter` chain). Previous lite implementation read `create_input_stream().read().decode("utf-8")` and returned `None` on any exception, which silently dropped non-UTF-8 / BOM-prefixed bodies. The new path matches upstream's text-string decoding contract while keeping pypdfbox's `None`-for-missing return (upstream returns `""`; we keep the explicit `None` so callers can distinguish "no entry" from "empty payload"). The COSString branch is unchanged.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/form/pd_variable_text.py`: extracted the `/RV`-style "text-or-stream" decode into a protected `_get_string_or_stream(base)` helper mirroring upstream's `protected final String getStringOrStream(COSBase)`. Callers under `pdmodel/interactive/form` can now reuse the same helper for `/V` / `/DV` (PDTextField field-value entries that also admit a stream payload per PDF 32000-1 §12.7.4.3) without re-writing the COSString/COSStream switch.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/form/pd_variable_text.py`: pypdfbox-only own-dictionary predicates `has_default_appearance()` (`/DA`), `has_default_style_string()` (`/DS`), `has_q()` (`/Q`), `has_rich_text_value()` (`/RV`). Each `contains_key` on the field's own dictionary — does not walk the inheritable chain. Lets callers distinguish "explicit /Q = 0 (left-aligned)" from "no /Q entry" (both return 0 via `get_q()`), and "field defines its own /DA" from "/DA inherited from AcroForm". Mirrors the predicate-pair pattern already established for `PDTextField.has_value` / `has_default_value` / `has_max_len`.

## PDAcroForm — set_need_appearances(None) + has_default_resources / has_default_appearance + get_q_if_exists

- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/form/pd_acro_form.py`: `set_need_appearances(value)` now accepts `None` and removes the `/NeedAppearances` entry. Upstream's signature is `setNeedAppearances(Boolean value)` — the boxed `Boolean` type allows `null`, which would NPE through `COSDictionary.setBoolean(null)` in 3.0.x; pypdfbox surfaces the spec-compliant "drop the entry" semantic explicitly so callers can clear `/NeedAppearances` without reaching into the dictionary.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/form/pd_acro_form.py`: pypdfbox-only predicates `has_default_resources()` (`/DR` resolves to a dictionary) and `has_default_appearance()` (`/DA` entry present). Cheaper than `get_default_resources() is not None` (skips the `PDResources` wrapper construction) and disambiguates "explicit empty `/DA`" from "no `/DA` entry" (both return `""` via `get_default_appearance()`). Mirrors the `has_xfa` / `has_fields` / `has_calc_order` predicate pattern already established on PDAcroForm.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/form/pd_acro_form.py`: pypdfbox-only `get_q_if_exists()` returns `/Q` as a tri-state (`None` when absent, otherwise the integer). Useful for writers that want to round-trip `/Q` without inventing the default `QUADDING_LEFT` (`0`). Mirrors the `get_default_appearance_if_exists` / `get_need_appearances_if_exists` pattern.

## PDMatrix — port of upstream `util/Matrix` re-homed under `pdmodel/common`

- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/common/pd_matrix.py`: port of `org.apache.pdfbox.util.Matrix` re-homed under `pdmodel/common` and renamed `PDMatrix`. Justification: this is the matrix type callers reach for from page-level objects (`/Matrix` entries on form/transparency XObjects, `/BBox` + `/Matrix` annotation appearance streams, page CTM serialisation) and lives more naturally next to `PDStream`/`PDRange` than under `pypdfbox.util` (which is otherwise empty in pypdfbox). Surface ported: 6-float and no-arg constructors, `create_matrix(COSBase)` factory, `get_scale_instance` / `get_translate_instance` / `get_rotate_instance` factories, `get_value` / `set_value` / `get_values` raw access, typed `get_scale_x` / `get_shear_y` / `get_shear_x` / `get_scale_y` / `get_translate_x` / `get_translate_y` / `get_scaling_factor_x` / `get_scaling_factor_y`, in-place `translate` / `scale` / `rotate` / `concatenate`, non-mutating `multiply`, static `concatenate_matrices(a, b)` (renamed from upstream `Matrix.concatenate(a,b)` to disambiguate from the instance method), `transform_point(x, y) -> (x, y)`, `to_cos_array()`, `clone()`. Not-finite guard on multiply/concatenate/translate/scale raises `ValueError` (upstream `IllegalArgumentException`). Deferred until `pypdfbox.util.Vector` and `pypdfbox.util.AffineTransform` land: `Matrix(AffineTransform)` constructor, `Matrix.translate(Vector)`, `Matrix.transform(Point2D)` / `Matrix.transform(Vector)`, `Matrix.createAffineTransform()`. pypdfbox extensions: `is_identity()` predicate, `get_single()` defensive copy of the flat 9-element array, `__copy__` / `__deepcopy__` hooks, value-equality / hashing, `__repr__` with the 6 named components.

## PDPageTree — pythonic membership + is_empty / has_page predicates + is_page_dict / get_parent statics

- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/pd_page_tree.py`: `__contains__` so `page in tree` resolves to `index_of(page) >= 0` for `PDPage` and raw `COSDictionary` operands; non-page operands short-circuit to `False`. `__bool__` mirrors `len(self) > 0`. Pypdfbox extensions — upstream Java `PDPageTree` has no membership / truthiness API but both protocols are the natural Pythonic spelling of the existing surface and avoid surprising `bool(tree) is True` for an empty tree under default object truthiness.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/pd_page_tree.py`: pypdfbox-only `is_empty()` (matches Java's `Collection.isEmpty()` idiom — equivalent to `len(self) == 0`) and `has_page(page)` (named alias for `index_of(page) >= 0`, accepts a `PDPage` or its backing `COSDictionary`). Mirrors the `has_*` predicate pattern used across pypdfbox (`PDTextField.has_value`, `PDVariableText.has_q`, `PDAcroForm.has_default_resources`).
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/pd_page_tree.py`: publicised the leaf-page detection and parent-resolution helpers as static methods. `is_page_dict(node)` exposes the lenient leaf detection (`/Type /Page` ⇒ True, `/Type /Pages` ⇒ False, untyped ⇒ True iff no `/Kids`) so callers porting upstream's free-floating `COSName.PAGE.equals(node.getCOSName(COSName.TYPE))` checks have a named entrypoint that complements the already-public `is_page_tree_node(node)`. `get_parent(node)` centralises upstream's `getCOSDictionary(PARENT, P)` two-key fallback so callers walking the tree don't repeat the dance; returns `None` when neither key resolves to a dictionary.

## Wave 274 — form XObject helpers, transparency-group predicates, structure categories

- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/graphics/form/pd_form_x_object.py`: added `SUBTYPE = "Form"` plus raw `/OPI` accessors and `has_opi()` / `has_metadata()` predicates so callers can identify form XObjects and inspect optional prepress / metadata entries without reaching into the backing COS dictionary.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/graphics/form/pd_transparency_group_attributes.py`: added mutable `/CS`, `/I`, `/K`, and `/S` helpers (`set_color_space`, `set_isolated`, `set_knockout`, `get_subtype`, `set_subtype`) plus presence predicates and `is_transparency_group()` for cheap explicit-vs-default checks.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/documentinterchange/logicalstructure/pd_structure_element.py`: added PDF 32000-1 standard-structure category predicates (`is_grouping_level`, `is_block_level`, `is_inline_level`, `is_illustration_level`) resolved through the existing role-map-aware standard type lookup.

## Wave 275 — logical-structure reference helpers and OCG usage predicates

- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/documentinterchange/logicalstructure/pd_marked_content_reference.py` / `pd_object_reference.py`: refreshed stale deferred notes now that typed `/Pg` accessors are present, added focused coverage for raw aliases, malformed-shape behavior, and reference predicates. `PDObjectReference.set_referenced_object()` now accepts only typed `PDAnnotation` or `PDXObject` wrappers; raw COS values continue to go through `set_obj()`.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/documentinterchange/logicalstructure/pd_structure_node.py` / `pd_attribute_object.py`: widened checkpoint coverage for kid insertion/count/membership and attribute add/remove/notify maintenance. Added `PDAttributeObject.has_owner()` and `has_revision_number()` to distinguish explicit entries from default getter values.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/graphics/optionalcontent/pd_optional_content_group_usage.py`: added `has_*` / `clear_*` helpers for each typed `/Usage` subdictionary (CreatorInfo, Language, Export, Zoom, Print, View, User, PageElement), keeping the existing get-or-create accessors intact.

## Wave 276 — graphics-state, outline, thread, and box-style checkpoint

- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/graphics/state/pd_soft_mask.py`: `get_group()` now threads the resource cache into the returned `PDFormXObject`, matching the wrapper's documented cache behavior. `PDFontSetting` coverage was widened and a stale test was corrected to use `COSArray.get()` when asserting the stored `COSNull` object (`get_object()` resolves it to `None`).
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/documentnavigation/outline/pd_outline_node.py`: added `remove_child()` convenience unlinking with `/First`/`/Last`, sibling, parent, and count maintenance, plus cycle protection for malformed outline chains.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/pagenavigation/pd_thread_bead.py` and `pypdfbox/pdmodel/documentinterchange/prepress/pd_box_style.py`: added snake_case next/previous bead aliases and allowed `set_guideline_width(None)` to clear `/W`; checkpoint tests now cover thread/bead linkage and box-style defaults/clearing/malformed-shape behavior.

## Wave 277 — interactive action checkpoint

- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/action/pd_action_uri.py` / `pd_uri_dictionary.py`: added explicit clearing helpers for URI map/base state (`clear_track_mouse_position`, `clear_base`) and widened tests around URI/base/default/malformed COS shapes.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/action/pd_action_submit_form.py` / `pd_action_import_data.py`: added form-action presence/clearing helpers (`has_file`, `clear_file`, `has_fields`, `clear_fields`, `has_flags`, `is_valid`, import-data `clear_file`) and made malformed non-file-spec `/F` entries resolve to `None` rather than being handed to file-spec dispatch.
- Launch, hide, named, JavaScript, and unknown action classes needed no production changes; Wave 277 adds focused coverage for their defaults, clearing behavior, string/stream payloads, COS round-trip, and malformed-entry tolerance.

## Wave 278 — annotation checkpoint

- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/annotation/pd_annotation_free_text.py`: malformed `/CL` and `/RD` arrays now return `None` instead of coercing bad COS entries; `set_line_ending(None)` / `set_line_ending_style(None)` explicitly clear `/LE`; border-effect docs now reflect the typed wrapper.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/annotation/pd_annotation_three_d.py`: added descriptive raw-entry aliases for 3D artwork, default view, and activation dictionaries (`get_artwork`, `get_default_view`, `get_activation_dictionary`, plus setters), keeping the raw `/3DD` / `/3DV` / `/3DA` accessors intact.
- Watermark, markup, and line annotations received stale-doc cleanup plus focused tests for defaults, clearing, malformed COS shapes, factory/COS round-trip, and inherited markup/border behavior.

## Wave 279 — font checkpoint

- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/font/pd_cid_system_info.py`: malformed single-argument construction now raises `TypeError` instead of silently creating an empty dictionary; valid no-arg, existing-dictionary, and `(registry, ordering, supplement)` forms are unchanged.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/font/pd_type3_font.py`: `set_font_matrix(None)`, `set_first_char(None)`, `set_last_char(None)`, and `set_widths(None)` now clear their COS entries, restoring the existing missing-entry defaults.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/font/pd_type1c_font.py`: `get_font_program()` now reads the embedded CFF `/FontFile3` program, making it symmetric with `set_font_program()`. Descriptor, simple-font, CID-system-info, Type3, and Type1C tests were widened around malformed COS shapes, explicit-vs-default behavior, and not-implemented paths.

## Wave 280 — function and shading checkpoint

- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/common/function/pd_function.py` / `pd_function_type0.py`: refreshed stale deferred-evaluation docs now that Type 0 and Type 4 evaluation exist, and tightened Type 0 `/Size` numeric validation while preserving malformed entries as invalid-size errors.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/common/function/pd_function_type3.py`: added `get_encode_values()`, made `get_encode_for_parameter(-1)` return `None`, and now raises a clear `ValueError` when `/Bounds` declares more partitions than `/Functions`.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/graphics/shading/pd_shading_type6.py` / `pd_shading_type7.py`: clarified that encoded patch streams are preserved while geometry decode remains deferred, added negative-index guards for decode pair access, and typed the optional `/Function` accessors.

## Wave 281 — destinations, patterns, file specs, navigation, labels/preferences

- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/documentnavigation/destination/*`: destination name-tree leaves now tolerate malformed explicit destinations by returning `None`; `/FitBH` and `/FitBV` gained missing clear/unset helpers for their coordinate slots. Existing destination tests now assert raw stored `COSNull` through `COSArray.get()` rather than the resolving `get_object()` helper.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/graphics/pattern/*`: added valid-entry `has_*` and explicit clear helpers for pattern matrix, ExtGState, shading, tiling BBox, and resources, with malformed arrays treated consistently as absent/defaulted.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/common/filespecification/*`: refreshed `/EF` cache behavior when the backing COS entry changes, added embedded-file slot predicates/clearers, and expanded `PDEmbeddedFile` `has_*` / `clear_*` helpers for params, subtype, dates, checksum, creator, and Mac metadata/resource-fork aliases.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/pagenavigation/*`: transitions, threads, and beads gained optional-entry `has_*` / `clear_*` helpers and alias coverage; malformed bead thread/rectangle values now resolve as absent instead of leaking parser details.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/pd_page_labels.py`, `pd_page_label_range.py`, and `pd_viewer_preferences.py`: removed stale page-label stub wording, added page-label-range clear helpers, made sparse labels respect explicit page-count overrides, added viewer-preference clear/presence helpers, and hardened `/PrintPageRange` validation against non-integer entries.

## Wave 282 — name trees, resources, operators, tagged PDF, and color

- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/common/pd_name_tree_node.py`: name-tree mutations now refresh ancestor `/Limits`, `clear_names()` / `clear_kids()` aliases were added, malformed `/Names` pairs with null-resolving values now raise a clear `OSError`, and `merge()` was cleaned for strict linting.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/pd_resources.py`: added `/ProcSet` presence/clearing plus clear helpers for named font, XObject, color-space, pattern, shading, ExtGState, and properties entries; typed XObject and malformed-category behavior is now documented and covered.
- `pypdfbox/contentstream/operator/*`: operator values gained inline-image predicates and clear/presence helpers for operands/image payloads; registry typing now accepts the structural processor surface, and selected color/text/graphics operators were tightened for strict type checking without changing rendering semantics.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/documentinterchange/taggedpdf/*`: standard/tagged attribute objects gained `clear_attribute` and per-key `has_*` / `clear_*` helpers for list, table, print-field, and export-format entries; malformed mixed arrays for name/number/four-colour helpers now resolve as absent instead of partially coercing.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/graphics/color/*`: calibrated, ICC, indexed, pattern, separation, and DeviceN wrappers gained presence/clear helpers for optional dictionary/array slots; stale deferred notes were refreshed to match implemented best-effort conversion paths, and sparse color-space arrays are now grown defensively before slot writes.

## Wave 283 — catalog, page model, page tree, info dictionary, and forms

- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/pd_document_catalog.py`: refreshed catalog docs, added valid-entry `has_*` probes for output intents, actions, layout/mode, base URI, and needs-rendering state, plus clear helpers for the major optional catalog entries and alias spellings.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/pd_page.py`: optional page entries now have explicit clear helpers and raw key-presence `has_*` probes for metadata, thumbnails, transitions, actions, annotations, beads, viewports, groups, tab order, and duration; setters delegate to the clear helpers.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/pd_page_tree.py`: traversal now uses the public kid-repair path, malformed non-name `/Type` values on pages normalize to `/Page`, and collection helpers `has_pages()` / `clear()` were added.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/pd_document_information.py`: standard/custom metadata getters now accept only real `COSString` values (except `/Trapped`, which remains lenient for names/strings), while new property presence/clear helpers preserve raw-key inspection behavior.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/form/*`: AcroForm and field wrappers gained stricter malformed-COS handling, new `has_*` / `clear_*` helpers for defaults, values, options, signatures, XFA, resources, actions, and related form slots, plus strict typing cleanup in appearance generation and flattening.

## Wave 284 — document lifecycle, loader, COS streams, parser, and writer

- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/pd_document.py`: refreshed document-surface docs, added trailer-level `has_*` / `clear_*` helpers for `/Root`, `/Info`, and `/Encrypt`, and tightened malformed trailer coverage while preserving `is_encrypted()` as the raw key-presence check.
- `pypdfbox/loader.py`: `load_fdf()` now delegates to `FDFDocument.load`, binary-stream detection rejects non-callable `read` attributes at the loader boundary, and partially-created parser documents are closed on parse failure.
- `pypdfbox/cos/cos_stream.py`: added filter and decode-parameter helpers (`set_filters`, `has_filter(s)`, `get_first_filter`, `get_filters_as_strings`, `clear_filters`, `get_decode_parms`, `clear_decode_parms`), fixed single-string `stop_filters`, and reject malformed non-name/non-array `/Filter` values instead of iterating dictionary keys.
- `pypdfbox/pdfparser/*`: `/Prev` xref-chain walking now follows the current section trailer rather than the merged trailer, and object-stream parsing shares validation for `/N`, `/First`, truncated headers, negative object numbers, and out-of-payload offsets.
- `pypdfbox/pdfwriter/*`: writer docs were refreshed for implemented xref/object-stream paths, inline-image parameter writing now skips malformed value-less entries before emitting dangling keys, `ContentStreamWriter` preserves `write_bytes` sink support with stricter typing, and `COSWriter` exposes `has_started_streams()` / `clear_started_streams()`.

## Wave 285 — filters, IO, CMap, TTF naming, and tools

- `pypdfbox/filter/*`: predictor geometry now rejects non-positive `/Columns`, `/Colors`, and `/BitsPerComponent` before row math; PNG prior rows are validated before applying Up/Average/Paeth filters; filter modules were tightened for strict typing without changing successful decode semantics.
- `pypdfbox/io/*`: random-access `available()` clamps at zero after permissive seeks, mmap construction closes the file descriptor on mapping failure, `ScratchFile.create_buffer_from_input()` is typed to random-access sources, and `ScratchFileBuffer.is_empty()` mirrors the write-buffer helper surface.
- `pypdfbox/fontbox/cmap/*`: CMap parsing now accepts `memoryview` sources and decodes malformed UTF-16BE bfchar/bfrange targets with replacement instead of aborting the parse; manager parse hooks use the shared source type.
- `pypdfbox/fontbox/ttf/naming_table.py`: name strings are read from the declared string-storage offset, with start/end bounds checked against both the table length and backing stream size before decoding malformed records.
- `pypdfbox/tools/*`: command modules were cleaned for strict ruff/mypy, `pdfdebugger` rejects negative object/generation ids consistently in CLI and walker paths, and page-size/filter constants now avoid dynamic COSName/PDRectangle attribute assumptions.

## Wave 286 — encryption, FDF, multipdf, text/rendering, and XMPBox

- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/encryption/*`: crypt-filter dictionaries gained typed `has_*` / `clear_*` helpers for `/CFM`, `/Length`, `/Recipients`, and `/EncryptMetadata`; `set_cfm(None)` clears `/CFM`, malformed crypt-filter entry types fall back to defaults, and public-key/security-handler typing was tightened.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/fdf/*`: catalog, dictionary, field, and common annotation wrappers gained optional-entry `has_*` / `clear_*` helpers; annotation rectangle/color parsing now rejects malformed numeric arrays instead of converting bad entries to `0.0`.
- `pypdfbox/multipdf/*`: merger stream-source handling now rejects non-callable `read` attributes and callable streams that return non-bytes payloads, while existing `memoryview` / `bytearray` source support is documented and covered.
- `pypdfbox/text/*` and `pypdfbox/rendering/*`: text extraction and rendering now accept already-typed cached `PDFont` resources directly, and `Tm` matrix operand handling rejects malformed/short numeric operands cleanly.
- `pypdfbox/xmpbox/*`: schemas and structured types gained raw/local property `has_property`, `clear_property`, and `clear` helpers that treat falsy stored values as present; XMPBox modules were cleaned for strict ruff/mypy without public behavior changes.

## Wave 287 — FontBox AFM, CFF, encoding, Type1, and GSUB

- `pypdfbox/fontbox/afm/afm_parser.py`: malformed `CharMetrics` directives now raise `OSError` for truncated fields and invalid numeric payloads instead of leaking `IndexError` / `ValueError`.
- `pypdfbox/fontbox/cff/*`: degenerate CFF charset parsing treats non-positive glyph counts as an empty charset without consuming the stream, and CFF helper modules were tightened for strict ruff/mypy.
- `pypdfbox/fontbox/encoding/*`: `GlyphList` now derives recognized `uXXXXX` / `uXXXXXX` Unicode glyph names and returns `None` for out-of-range synthesized code points rather than raising from `chr()`.
- `pypdfbox/fontbox/type1/*`: malformed negative `RD` charstring lengths now produce an empty charstring token without rewinding lexer state; truncated payload handling remains tolerant.
- `pypdfbox/fontbox/ttf/gsub/*`: the `GsubData.NO_DATA_FOUND` sentinel is declared as a typed `ClassVar`, stale ignores were removed, and `get_supported_features()` copy behavior is covered.

## Wave 288 — COS arrays, common streams, image wrappers, measurement/sound, and signatures

- `pypdfbox/cos/cos_array.py`: typed convenience accessors and list converters now resolve indirect `COSObject` entries through a shared resolver, treating indirect `COSNull` and unresolved references as absent while preserving raw `get()` behavior.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/common/pd_stream.py`: binary embedding now requires non-bytes sources to expose a callable `read()` returning bytes-like data, and file-like sources are closed even when read-result validation fails.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/graphics/image/*`: image decode/matte readers now reject malformed numeric arrays instead of coercing bad entries to `0.0`; image wrappers gained optional-entry clear helpers, including short/long alias clearing for inline-image color space, filter, and decode entries.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/measurement/*` and `sound/*`: number-format dictionaries and sound streams gained typed `has_*` / `clear_*` helpers, with malformed COS entries falling through getter defaults while presence helpers report them absent.
