Includes the PDFBOX-5987 `num denom div` collapse, `flex1` `dx_is_bigger` selector, and deprecated `seac` 4-arg `endchar` form. `convert_type1_to_type2` populates the inherited Type 1 sequence buffer for upstream-shape parity, but path / width interpretation stays delegated to fontTools' `T2CharString` rather than the Java `GeneralPath` renderer (library-first rule).
- `pypdfbox/fontbox/cmap/cmap_parser.py`: added `_get_external_cmap` and `_read_until_end_of_line` helpers to centralize what was inlined in `_parse_usecmap` and `_read_line`. **Bug fix:** array-form `bfrange` length guard relaxed from `>= end - start + 1` to `>= end - start`, matching upstream `CMapParser.java:407` — the prior stricter check rejected off-by-one CMaps that upstream tolerates. Imported five upstream cmap fixtures (`CMapTest`, `CMapNoWhitespace`, `CMapMalformedbfrange1`, `CMapMalformedbfrange2`, `Identitybfrange`) and added 7 ported parity tests.
- `pypdfbox/fontbox/ttf/glyph_substitution_table.py`: surfaced 12 structural accessors that upstream keeps private — `get_lookup_count`, `get_lookup_types`, `get_script_list`, `get_feature_list`, `get_lookup_list`, `get_lookup`, `get_lookup_subtables`, `get_feature_record`, `get_lang_sys_tables`, `get_feature_records` (with required-feature inclusion + `vrt2`/`vert` supersession + enabled-features ordering), `select_script_tag`, `get_last_used_supported_script`, `apply_feature`, `do_lookup`. Also exposes 8 `LOOKUP_TYPE_*` class constants matching the OT spec, mirroring the GPOS sibling.
- `pypdfbox/fontbox/type1/type1_parser.py`: added `EEXEC_KEY` / `CHARSTRING_KEY` constants (Java lines 44–45), `is_binary` (958), `hex_to_binary` (978), `decrypt` (927), `read(kind, name=None)` (covering both Java overloads at 881 / 895), `read_maybe` (910), `read_value` / `read_dict_value` / `read_proc` / `read_proc_void` / `read_post_script_wrapper`, `read_def` / `read_put`, `read_simple_dict` / `read_simple_value` / `read_font_info` / `read_encoding` / `read_private` / `read_other_subrs`, `array_to_numbers`. Top-level `parse()` retains the existing tolerant streaming extractor; new method-level helpers raise `OSError` on violations matching upstream `IOException` shape.

## Wave 398 — fontbox TTF + encryption + appearance handler 1:1 parity round-out

- `pypdfbox/fontbox/ttf/ttf_parser.py`: added `parse_table_headers(source)` matching `TTFParser.parseTableHeaders` (Java lines 114 / 260), `_read_table(tag)` hook for unknown tags (line 403), and a full `FontHeaders` class mirroring `FontHeaders.java` (15 getters/setters incl. `set_otf_ros`, `BYTES_GCID`). Non-fatal validation errors surface via `set_error()` rather than raising, matching upstream.
- `pypdfbox/fontbox/ttf/ttf_subsetter.py`: surfaced `should_copy_name_record(record)` matching upstream's private `shouldCopyNameRecord` filter (line 301). **Bug fix:** `_apply_prefix` was tagging both nameID 4 (full name) and nameID 6 (PostScript name); upstream's `buildNameTable` (line 359) tags only nameID 6 — pypdfbox now matches.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/encryption/standard_security_handler.py`: added byte[]-args overloads for `is_user_password` / `is_owner_password` (Java lines 1013 / 1086 / 592 / 1118), `compute_encrypted_key` 11-arg form with r5/r6 unwrap dispatch (725), `truncate_127` (1255), `truncate_or_pad` (987), `adjust_user_key` with "Bad U length" error (1218), `compute_sha_256` (1210), `compute_hash_2a` (1127), `compute_hash_2b` (1136), `compute_rc_4_key` with PDFBOX-6115 catch (951), instance `validate_perms(encryption, dic_permissions, encrypt_metadata)` log-on-mismatch (317), `_get_document_id_bytes` accepting both COSArray and bytes (298), `_compute_encryption_key_rev_5_6` r5/r6 unwrap helper (783). `compute_user_password` now returns `b""` for r5/r6 (parity with Java 862–865); `compute_owner_password` raises for r2 with length != 5 (920–923). **Security fix:** `/U` and `/O` validators now use `hmac.compare_digest` instead of `==`, closing a timing-side-channel divergence vs upstream's `MessageDigest.isEqual`.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/annotation/handlers/pd_abstract_appearance_handler.py`: rewrote against upstream surface — added `get_default_font` (Java 84), `get_color` (98), public `get_normal_appearance` returning entry (479), `get_down_appearance` (174), `get_rollover_appearance` (197), `set_opacity` via `PDExtendedGraphicsState` + `set_graphics_state_parameters` (273), `draw_style` (300), `draw_arrow` (359), `draw_diamond` (382), `draw_circle` (401), `draw_circle2` (424), `handle_border_box` (520), private `_set_transformation_matrix` (511), and class constants `ARROW_ANGLE` (61), `SHORT_STYLES` (59), `INTERIOR_COLOR_STYLES` (66), `ANGLED_STYLES` (71). **Bug fix:** `get_padded_rectangle`/`add_rect_differences`/`apply_rect_differences` now use `PDRectangle.from_xywh` to preserve upstream `(x, y, width, height)` semantics — the Python `PDRectangle` 4-arg constructor takes upper-right corners, so the prior helpers were silently mis-typed.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/font/pd_type1c_font.py`: added `get_font_box_font` (Java 162), enriched `get_path` with `.notdef` suppress + `sfthyphen→hyphen` + `nbspace→space` rewrites (178), `has_glyph_for_code` (201), `get_path_for_code` (217), `get_normalized_path_for_code` with `.notdef` fallback (237), `get_bounding_box` (descriptor `/FontBBox` first, CFF FontBBox fallback) (274), `get_font_matrix` reading CFF Top DICT (332), `get_width_from_font` rescaling CFF advance to 1/1000 em (369), `get_string_width` summing CFF advances per code point (434).

> **Wave numbering merge (2026-05-09):** prior CHANGES.md wave numbers (last: Wave 398) and the parallel coverage-wave counter (last: 1237) were merged into a single shared counter in `.parity/wave_counter.txt`. The next wave number is **1238**. The gap between Wave 398 and Wave 1238 is intentional and documents the merger.

## Wave 1238 — DateConverter + OTFParser + KerningSubtable + CmapSubtable + FDSelect 1:1 parity round-out

- `pypdfbox/xmpbox/date_converter.py`: added snake_case ports of upstream `pdfbox.util.DateConverter` internal helpers (`to_string`, `restrain_t_zoffset`, `format_t_zoffset`, `parse_time_field`, `skip_optionals`, `skip_string`, `new_greg`, `adjust_time_zone_nicely`, `parse_t_zoffset`, `update_zone_id`, `parse_big_endian_date`, `parse_simple_date`, `parse_date`, `from_iso8601`) plus `_GregLike` Calendar-shaped mutable bag and a Java `ParsePosition` shim. `parse_simple_date` is partial: regex-driven for digit-start `SimpleDateFormat` patterns; alpha-start patterns (need full locale month/weekday dictionaries) fall through. Existing public `to_calendar` / `to_iso8601` semantics unchanged.
- `pypdfbox/fontbox/ttf/otf_parser.py`: surfaced `parse(...)` covariant override returning `OpenTypeFont` (Java line 48), `new_font(data)` factory (60), `read_table(tag)` switch over BASE/GDEF/GPOS/GSUB/JSTF/CFF (66), `allow_cff()` (85). Legacy `_new_font` / `_allow_cff` / `_read_table` remain as forwarding shims for in-repo callers. Parity 0% → 57% (4/7); the remaining 3 are parity-script false positives (`new CFFTable()` constructor calls misread as method signatures).
- `pypdfbox/fontbox/ttf/kerning_subtable.py`: surfaced `read_subtable0`, `read_subtable1`, `read_subtable0_format0`, `read_subtable0_format2` (Java lines 180 / 233 / 222 / 228), plus static `is_bits_set(bits, mask, shift)` and `get_bits(bits, mask, shift)` (lines 238 / 243). Rewired `_read_subtable_0` coverage decoding to call `is_bits_set` / `get_bits` directly, matching upstream. Parity 30% → 90%.
- `pypdfbox/fontbox/ttf/cmap_subtable.py`: promoted underscored format dispatchers to public names matching upstream — `process_subtype0..14` (Java lines 580 / 493 / 397 / 370 / 132 / 215 / 240 / 298 / 356), `build_glyph_id_to_character_code_lookup` (459), `new_glyph_id_to_character_code` (597), `get_char_code` (649), and added `to_string` (692) which `__repr__` now delegates to. Underscored aliases retained for in-repo callers. **Stripped** all `# noqa: N802` camelCase aliases (`getPlatformId`, `setPlatformId`, `getPlatformEncodingId`, `setPlatformEncodingId`, `getGlyphId`, `getCharCode`, `getCharCodes`, `getGlyphIdUVS`, `hasUVS`) per the global no-camelCase-aliases rule. Tests updated to use snake_case.
- `pypdfbox/fontbox/cff/fd_select.py`: added `__repr__` for `Format0FDSelect` and `Format3FDSelect` mirroring upstream `toString()` (CFFParser.java lines 1160 / 1109). `Range3` is rendered with the bare class name `Range3[...]` rather than upstream's `getClass().getName()` (which would embed the synthetic `CFFParser$Range3` Java inner-class prefix) — keeps repr stable across re-syncs.

## Wave 1239 — multipdf + shading + image factory + xmpbox 1:1 parity round-out

- `pypdfbox/multipdf/splitter.py`: surfaced `clone_structure_tree`, `clone_id_tree`, `clone_role_map`, `clone_tree_element`, `fix_destinations`, `process_resources` as public methods (mirror upstream Java private names at lines 193 / 252 / 281 / 300 / 159 / 586) with leading-underscore back-compat aliases. Structure-tree clone now walks page resources and appearance-stream resources for XObject `/StructParent[s]` (matching upstream lines 229–235) — was missing. Parity 37.5% → 100%.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/graphics/image/ccitt_factory.py`: rewrote with full byte-level TIFF IFD walker. Added `read_short`, `read_long`, `extract_from_tiff`, `prepare_image_x_object`, `create_from_random_access_impl`, plus `number` argument on `create_from_byte_array` / `create_from_file` (Java lines 470 / 479 / 235 / 137 / 209 / 128 / 190). Multi-page TIFFs and `FillOrder=2` LSB-first streams now parse byte-for-byte like upstream. **Bug fix:** `/BlackIs1` polarity now matches upstream Java (`if val == 1` at Java line 354) — the prior inverted convention silently flipped the bit polarity. Stripped `createFromImage` / `createFromByteArray` / `createFromFile` camelCase aliases (no-aliases rule). Static-only utility class enforces it via raising `__init__`.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/graphics/shading/pd_shading_type6.py` and `pd_shading_type7.py`: added `to_paint(matrix=None)`, `generate_patch(points, color)` (validating 12-point Coons / 16-point Tensor arity per PDF 32000-1 §8.7.4.5.7-8 and 4-corner color array), and `get_bounds(xform=None, matrix=None)` (Java lines 50 / 56 / 62). `generate_patch` returns `{"kind": "coons"|"tensor", "points": ..., "color": ...}` descriptor as a deferred-rendering placeholder; `to_paint` returns `None` since Pillow renderer doesn't use AWT `Paint`.
- `pypdfbox/xmpbox/type/type_mapping.py`: substantial expansion. Added `PropertyType` dataclass, `PropertiesDescription` class, `DefinedStructuredType`, lightweight `_SchemaFactory`, plus methods `add_to_defined_structured_types`, `get_defined_description_by_namespace` (1-arg / 2-arg upstream overloads), `get_structured_prop_mapping`, `get_schema_factory`, `add_new_namespace` (now creates a factory), `get_specified_property_type` with full PDFBOX-6133 logic, `instanciate_simple_field`, `instanciate_defined_type`, static `initialize_prop_mapping(cls)` harvesting `_FIELD_TYPES` + `PROPERTIES`, static `create_property_type` (Java lines 144 / 162 / 176 / 280 / 316 / 274 / 339 / 236 / 210 / 418 / 529). Reflection-based `@PropertyType` field scanning replaced by class-attribute conventions (`_FIELD_TYPES` / `PROPERTIES`). `BadFieldValueException` extends `ValueError` so historical pypdfbox callers still work. Schema-factory entries are lightweight namespace+`PropertiesDescription` records — no `XMPSchema` instantiation (the parser-side wiring TypeMapping needs today doesn't require it).
- `pypdfbox/multipdf/overlay.py`: no changes — investigation revealed the parity script's "0%" reading for the `Position` enum is a false positive (the script attributed `Overlay`'s outer-class methods to the inner enum). PDFBOX-6048 lower-left-corner deviation in `_calculate_affine_transform` (existing CHANGES note) preserved.

## Wave 1240 — fontbox + font + multipdf + digitalsignature 1:1 parity round-out

- `pypdfbox/fontbox/cmap/cmap_parser.py`: dropped leading underscore on Java-private parity surface (`parse_next_token`, `parse_integer`, `parse_byte_array`, `read_array`, `read_string`, `read_line`, `read_until_end_of_line`, `read_literal_name`, `read_operator`, `read_number`, `read_dictionary`, `parse_usecmap`, `parse_literal_name`, `check_expected_operator`, `parse_begincodespacerange`, `parse_beginbfchar`, `parse_beginbfrange`, `parse_begincidchar`, `parse_begincidrange`, plus class-level statics `is_whitespace_or_eof`, `is_delimiter`, `increment`, `create_string_from_bytes`). Unified the two `addMappingFrombfrange` Java overloads (lines 444 / 454) under a single Python signature with optional `token_bytes`. Module-level `_increment` / `_is_whitespace_or_eof` / etc. retained as thin shims for prior wave tests. Parity 6.5% → 87.1%.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/digitalsignature/cos_filter_input_stream.py`: added `to_byte_array` (Java line 81), `calculate_ranges(byte_range)` (line 86, exposed as a pure helper without mutating instance state), `get_remaining` (96), `next_range` (101).
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/font/pd_true_type_font.py`: added `is_embedded()` override (line 487 — **bug fix:** was inheriting `PDFont.is_embedded` which returns `True` for an empty `/FontFile2`; upstream returns `False` on parse failure, now matched), `get_bounding_box()` + `_generate_bounding_box()` (349 / 358) with caching, `get_width_from_font(code)` (388) `hmtx`-scaled to 1000 units, `get_normalized_path(code)` (560) with the GID-0/non-embedded/non-Standard14 guard, `extract_cmap_table()` (734) with PDFBOX-4755 / PDFBOX-5484 Unicode-platform aliasing, `read_encoding_from_font()` (283) synthesising `BuiltInEncoding` from the `post` table, `encode_codepoint(unicode)` (413).
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/font/standard14_fonts.py`: added `map_name(alias, base_name=None)` (Java lines 153 / 165, two overloads merged via optional `base_name`), `load_metrics(name)` (129, canonical-only — rejects aliases matching upstream `FontName`-typed signature), `get_mapped_font(base_name)` (245, cached in module-level `_GENERIC_FONTS_CACHE` mirroring upstream's `GENERIC_FONTS` `EnumMap`), `get_glyph_list(base_name)` (309), `get_glyph_path(base_name, glyph_name)` (271, four-step lookup: notdef short-circuit / direct / AGL `uniXXXX` / `SymbolMT` PUA fallback). `get_glyph_path` returns `[]` for unknown font name rather than raising — drawing nothing matches the upstream end-state for a missing glyph and avoids aborting rendering.
- `pypdfbox/multipdf/pdf_clone_utility.py`: renamed 6 private helpers to public names so the parity audit recognises them as 1:1 ports — `clone_cos_base_for_new_document` (Java line 107), `clone_cos_array` (128), `clone_cos_stream` (146), `clone_cos_dictionary` (170), `clone_merge_cos_base` (206), `has_self_reference` (247). Underscore-prefixed back-compat shims kept so `pdf_merger_utility.py` and prior-wave tests still work. Parity now 9/9 = 100%.

## Wave 1241 — color spaces + xobject + structure attribute 1:1 parity round-out

- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/graphics/color/pd_color_space.py`: added `_create_from_cos_object(color_space, resources)` (Java line 244 — routes COSObject inputs through the resource cache so repeated dispatches short-circuit; `create()` now delegates COSObject handling here instead of inline), `to_rgb(value)` (line 306 — public concrete method delegating to `PDColor.to_rgb` so subclasses without their own override still satisfy the contract), `to_rgb_image_awt(raster, awt_color_space, width, height)` (line 353, surface-compat stub delegating to `to_rgb_image`), `to_raw_image_awt(raster, awt_color_space, width, height)` (line 339, surface-compat stub).
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/graphics/color/pd_icc_based.py`: added `create(icc_array, resources)` factory with resource-cache lookup (Java lines 108–132), `_check_array(icc_array)` validator (134–144), `is_srgb()` folding in private `is_sRGB(profile)` header inspection (246–252 / 540–543), `get_color_space_type()` returning AWT-style `ColorSpace.TYPE_*` integer (468–488), `set_alternate_color_spaces(alternates)` list-form setter (495–503), `__str__()` (545–549), plus module constants `TYPE_GRAY`/`TYPE_RGB`/`TYPE_CMYK`/etc. (AWT integer values, no AWT dependency). `is_srgb()` and `get_color_space_type()` read the ICC profile header bytes directly (no `java.awt.color.ICC_Profile` dependency); fall back to `/Alternate` name and `/N` inference matching upstream's alternate-CS path.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/graphics/color/pd_indexed.py`: added `to_rgb(value)` (Java line 173, one-component clamp + RGB-table dereference; raises `ValueError` for non-singleton input), `to_rgb_image(raster, width, height)` (194, library-first via Pillow `P`-mode + `convert("RGB")`; clamps indices to `actualMaxIndex`), `to_raw_image(raster, width, height)` (219), private `_read_lookup_data` (258), `_read_color_table` (285, returns `(table, actual_max_index)`), `_init_rgb_color_table` (128). `to_raw_image` always palette-decodes to an RGB Pillow image rather than upstream's `IndexColorModel`-backed `BufferedImage` — pypdfbox callers expect a Pillow image regardless of base CS. Per CLAUDE.md 4.0 alignment note, deprecated `setHighValue` (Java line 330) skipped — `set_hival` is the canonical name.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/graphics/pd_x_object.py`: added `__init__(stream: PDDocument, subtype)` overload (Java lines 113–119, mirrors upstream `protected PDXObject(PDDocument, COSName)` — creates a fresh `PDStream(document)` and stamps `/Type /XObject` + `/Subtype`). Existing `PDStream` and `COSStream` overloads (92–112) preserved.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/documentinterchange/logicalstructure/pd_attribute_object.py`: added `notify_changed()` (Java lines 185–191, upstream-faithful snake_case form; existing `notify_change` kept as compatibility alias), `potentially_notify_changed(old_base, new_base)` (156–162, promoted from subclass-private `_potentially_notify_changed` to base class), static `is_value_changed(old, new)` (172–179, static so tests can call without an instance), `to_string()` (194–197, delegates to `__str__`). Owner comparison now mirrors upstream `equals()` exactly — `is_value_changed` returns `True` for distinct types (e.g., `COSInteger.get(1)` vs `COSString("1")`).

## Wave 1242 — Type1CharString + COSWriter + SecurityHandler + Type1 font + ShadingType5 1:1 parity

- `pypdfbox/fontbox/cff/type1_char_string.py`: added `render` (Java line 142), `handle_type1_command` (160) full opcode dispatcher, `set_current_point` (331), `call_other_subr` (340) flex begin/end with 7-point flush, `rmove_to` (392), `rline_to` (403) with warn-and-recover when no current point, `rrcurve_to` (422) with warn-and-recover, `close_char_string1_path` (446) emits closepath + trailing moveto, `seac` (465) using `StandardEncoding` + parent's `get_type1_char_string`, `to_string` (539). Helpers operate on a `_RenderContext` rather than self-mutating, so the fontTools-driven render path stays reentrant. Parity 38.9% → 94.4%.
- `pypdfbox/pdfwriter/cos_writer.py`: added 22 upstream-spelled public/protected wrappers — `add_object_to_write` (Java 541), `add_x_ref_entry` (351), `prepare_increment` (325), `get_object_key` (1078), static `is_need_to_be_updated` (619), `detect_possible_signature` (1278), `do_write_header` (650), `do_write_body` (432), `do_write_body_compressed` (463), `do_write_objects` (533), `do_write_trailer` (684), `do_write_x_ref_table` (764), `do_write_x_ref_inc` (716), `do_write_increment` (863), `fill_gaps_with_free_entries` (812), `write_xref_range` (998), `write_xref_entry` (1006), `write_array` (1165), `write_dictionary` (1178), `visit_from_int` (1372) alias to `visit_from_integer`. Signing-pipeline writer-level hooks `get_data_to_sign` (943), `write_external_signature` (970), `do_write_signature` (874) raise `RuntimeError` until security cluster ports the digest pipeline (mirrors upstream `IllegalStateException`); `PDDocument.add_signature` is the supported entry. New `_reached_signature` field for parity with upstream's private `reachedSignature`.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/encryption/security_handler.py`: added `set_string_filter_name` / `get_string_filter_name` (Java line 159), `set_stream_filter_name` / `get_stream_filter_name` (169), `set_custom_secure_random` (179) / `get_secure_random` (436), `prepare_document_for_encryption` (191), `decrypt(COSBase, ...)` dispatch (476), `decrypt_string_if_absent` (454), `decrypt_stream_if_absent` (507), `decrypt_dictionary` (623), `decrypt_array` (727), `decrypt_stream_in_place` (527), `prepare_aes_initialization_vector` (404), `create_cipher` (393), `encrypt_data_rc4` (285 / 301), `encrypt_data_aes256` (358), `compute_version_number` (858), `has_protection_policy` (804), `get_protection_policy` (814), `set_protection_policy` (823), `calc_final_key` alias (248) → `compute_object_key`. `_protection_policy` slot lifted from subclass to base; identity-filter short-circuit and PDFBOX-2936 (skip `/CF` dicts) and PDFBOX-4477 (`IdentityHashMap` of seen objects) now mirror upstream. Parity 41.0% → 89.7%. `decrypt(COSBase)` and `decrypt_stream_in_place` are tolerant on missing `get_raw_bytes` / `set_raw_bytes` accessors (silent skip) — parity with upstream's catch-and-log behaviour.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/font/pd_type1_font.py`: added `repair_length1` (Java 310–336), `repair_length2` (373–382), static `find_binary_offset_after_exec` (338–361), `get_bounding_box` (556–563), `generate_bounding_box` (565–576), `get_font_matrix` (681–711), `get_font_box_font` (544–547) alias of `get_type1_font`, `get_normalized_path(int)` (657–666) alias of `get_normalized_path_for_code`, `read_encoding_from_font` (511–531) mirroring PDFBOX-2350 / 3475 / 3677. Cache fields `_bbox_cache` / `_font_matrix_cache` mirroring upstream's `fontBBox` / `fontMatrix` final fields.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/graphics/shading/pd_shading_type5.py`: added `to_paint(matrix=None)` (Java 81, lite-surface stub returning `None`), `collect_triangles(xform=None, matrix=None)` (88, validates COSStream backing, `/Decode` x/y ranges with degenerate-range short-circuit, `/VerticesPerRow`, per-component `/Decode` ranges; mesh stream decoding deferred to rendering cluster), `create_shaded_triangle_list(row_num, num_per_row, lattice_array)` (157, emits `2*(rowNum-1)*(numPerRow-1)` triangles in upstream's exact `(v1,v2,v3)+(v2,v3,v4)` ordering).

## Wave 1243 — PDType3CharProc + RandomAccessReadBuffer + COSParser + PDShadingType4 + PDDeviceCMYK 1:1 parity

- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/font/pd_type3_char_proc.py`: added `get_cos_object()` (Java 60), exposed `parse_width(operator, arguments)` mirroring upstream's private `parseWidth` (186), renamed `get_bbox` → `get_b_box` and `get_glyph_bbox` → `get_glyph_b_box` (Java 101 / 113) with old names retained as aliases. **Bug fix:** `get_content_stream()` now returns a fresh `PDStream` wrapper (matches upstream Java 70) — previously returned the underlying `COSStream` directly. `get_width` and `parse_width` keep the lenient pypdfbox behaviour of returning `0.0` for malformed/empty streams instead of raising `IOException` (rendering robustness across char-proc tables).
- `pypdfbox/io/random_access_read_buffer.py`: added `check_closed` (Java 344), `expand_buffer` no-op under BytesIO (308), `next_buffer` (329), `read_remaining_bytes` (260), `reset_buffers` (411), `is_eof` (366), `create_view` (372) with per-thread copy cache via `threading.get_ident()` mirroring upstream's `rarbCopies` ConcurrentMap. **Bug fix:** `chunk_size` now reflects buffer length when constructed from bytes/memoryview (matches upstream PDFBOX-5764 fix where the ctor uses the buffer's limit, not its capacity); previously hard-coded to 4096. `close()` now fans out `closeQuietly` over `_rarb_copies` before clearing.
- `pypdfbox/pdfparser/cos_parser.py`: added 20 upstream-spelled methods — `init` (Java 205, env-property hook for `SYSPROP_EOFLOOKUPRANGE`), `get_startxref_offset` (497), `parse_start_xref` (1472), `parse_trailer` (1537), `parse_xref` (334) chain walker w/ `/Prev` loop detection, `parse_xref_obj_stream` (465), `get_object_offset` (690), `parse_file_object` (717), `get_length` (854) `/Length` resolver, `read_until_end_stream` (983), `validate_stream_length` (1077), `check_x_ref_offset` (1121), `check_x_ref_stream_offset` (1156), `calculate_x_ref_fixed_offset` (1205), `validate_xref_offsets` (1223), `check_xref_offsets` (1278), `find_object_key` (1305), `get_brute_force_parser` (1388, returns `self` since pypdfbox inlines brute-force scans), `parse_header` (1617, renamed from `_parse_header_marker`), `check_pages_dictionary` (1420). Parity 45.2% → 92.9%.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/graphics/shading/pd_shading_type4.py`: added `to_paint(matrix=None)` (Java 86, lite-surface stub returning `None`) and `collect_triangles(xform=None, matrix=None)` (92, validates `/Decode` x/y ranges and per-component decode ranges raising `OSError` per upstream `IOException`; mesh decoding deferred to rendering cluster). Mirrors PDShadingType5 lite-surface convention.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/graphics/color/pd_device_cmyk.py`: added `init()` (Java 63, idempotent latch), `get_icc_profile()` (97, returns `None` since pypdfbox doesn't bundle `CGATS001Compat-v2-micro.icc`), `to_rgb_image()` (156, Pillow `'CMYK'` → `'RGB'` library-first conversion), `to_rgb_image_awt()` (163, delegates to `to_rgb_image`). **Bug fix:** `to_raw_image()` now returns `None` matching upstream — previously inherited the base class's CMYK Pillow image.

## Wave 1244 — PDOutlineNode + PDType3Font + PDCIDFontType2 + PDStructureNode + PDType0Font 1:1 parity

- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/documentnavigation/outline/pd_outline_node.py`: promoted underscore-prefixed package-private methods to public snake_case — `set_parent` (Java 67), `get_outline_item` (175), `set_first_child` (194), `set_last_child` (212), `switch_node_count` (264), `update_parent_open_count` (284), `require_single_node` (105), `append` (119), `prepend` (141), `update_parent_open_count_for_added_child` (157). Underscore aliases retained as class-level `_x = x`. **Bug fix:** PDFBOX-5939 self-parent guard now actually emits `LOG.warning("Outline parent points to itself")` matching upstream line 292; previously a silent comment. Parity 48% → 88%.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/font/pd_type3_font.py`: added `read_code(input_stream)` (Java 249, raises `EOFError` at EOF), `read_encoding()` (96, primes typed-encoding + glyph-list cache), `read_encoding_from_font()` (116, raises `NotImplementedError`), `get_path(name)` (129, raises), `get_font_box_font()` (143, raises), `encode_codepoint(unicode)` (243, raises with upstream message `"Not implemented: Type3"`), `is_standard14()` (280, alias of existing `is_standard_14`), private `_check_font_matrix_values(matrix)` (266), reworked `get_bounding_box()` and `_generate_bounding_box()` (315 / 324, memoised; falls back to CharProcs union when `/FontBBox` is all-zero matching upstream's plan-B). **Bug fix:** `get_name()` now reads `/Name` (Java 92) instead of inheriting `/BaseFont` from `PDFont` — matches upstream `PDType3Font.getName`.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/font/pd_cid_font_type2.py`: added `get_path(code)` (Java 437, routes through CFF or TTF `glyf`), `get_path_from_outlines(code)` (489, CFF/CFF2 charstring path gated on `is_open_type_post_script()`), `encode(unicode_codepoint)` (354, four-step resolution: Identity-H via TTF cmap → predefined-CMap UCS-2 → `/ToUnicode` raw codes → non-embedded TTF cmap; raises `ValueError` on no-glyph vs Java's `IllegalArgumentException`), `find_font_or_substitute()` (156, returns `None` fail-soft when `DefaultFontMapper` cannot substitute, where upstream's mapper would throw if no fallback is wired up).
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/documentinterchange/logicalstructure/pd_structure_node.py`: promoted four protected helpers from `_x` to public — `append_objectable_kid` (Java 161), `remove_objectable_kid` (297), `insert_objectable_before` (220), `create_object_from_dic` (395). `create_object` now delegates to `create_object_from_dic` mirroring upstream structure exactly. All callers updated; no aliases left behind.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/font/pd_type0_font.py`: added `get_path(code)` (Java 722) delegating to descendant `get_glyph_path`, `get_normalized_path(code)` (729), `get_gsub_data()` (745) surfacing descendant TTF GSUB, `get_cmap_lookup()` (766) exposing embedded TTF unicode cmap, `get_standard14_width(code)` (556) raises `NotImplementedError`, `has_explicit_width(code)` (518) overrides base, `add_glyphs_to_subset(glyph_ids)` (326) registers raw GIDs, `will_be_subset()` (351) overrides base, `load(doc, source, embed_subset)` (185–254) upstream-named alias dispatching to `load_ttf`, `load_vertical(doc, source, embed_subset)` (265–314) sets `/Encoding /Identity-V`, `read_encoding()` (359, public force-parse trigger). New `_subset_glyph_ids` and `_will_be_subset` fields; `subset()` consumes glyph_ids via `TTFSubsetter.add_glyph_ids` and clears the subset flag. **Bug fix:** `load_ttf` / `load_otf` now honour the `embed_subset` flag at load time — previously silently dropped via `del`.

## Wave 1245 — LayerUtility + OpenTypeFont + PDLab + PDCIDFont 1:1 parity

- `pypdfbox/multipdf/layer_utility.py`: promoted private helpers `transfer_dict` (Java 288) and `import_oc_properties` (308) to public snake_case so the parity audit recognises them as 1:1 ports.
- `pypdfbox/fontbox/ttf/open_type_font.py`: added `set_version(version_value)` (Java 42, records `_has_post_script_tag` via 0x469EA8A9 OTTO IEEE-754 fingerprint), `get_glyph_table()` override (66, raises `NotImplementedError` for PostScript-flavoured fonts), `get_path(name)` route (76, routes name lookups through CFF Type 2 charstrings when supported). **Bug fix:** `is_post_script()` previously hardcoded `True`; now matches upstream line 94 (OTTO magic OR `CFF`/`CFF2` table presence). **Bug fix:** `is_supported_otf()` previously checked only `has_table("CFF ")`; now matches upstream line 108 (rejects only OTTO + CFF2 + no `CFF`). TrueType outlines in OTF wrappers now correctly report supported. `get_cff()` returns `None` for non-PostScript fonts (upstream throws `UnsupportedOperationException`).
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/graphics/color/pd_lab.py`: added static `get_default_range_array` (Java 184), static `inverse` (140), `set_component_range_array` (246, public delegate), `to_rgb(value)` (122, Lab→XYZ→sRGB using the dictionary's `/WhitePoint` — parity with upstream's `wpX/wpY/wpZ` cache instead of the hardcoded D65 used by `PDColor._lab_to_rgb`), `to_rgb_image(raster, w, h)` (65, per-pixel L∈0..100, a/b scaled by `/Range`), `to_raw_image(raster, w, h)` (115, returns `None` per upstream). **Bug fix:** `PDLab.to_rgb` now respects the dictionary `/WhitePoint`; previously relied solely on `PDColor._lab_to_rgb`'s hardcoded D65 white-point. Parity 53.3% → 93.3%.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/font/pd_cid_font.py`: added `get_width_for_cid(cid)` (Java 274, alias of existing `get_glyph_width`), `read_widths()` (81, refreshes the cache), `read_vertical_displacements()` (146, re-parses `/W2`), and `@abstractmethod` stubs for `code_to_gid(code)` (405), `encode_glyph_id(glyph_id)` (407), `encode(unicode_codepoint)` (419) mirroring upstream's abstract surface. Parity 54.5% → 81.8%.

> **Note:** wave 1245's RandomAccessReadBufferedFile rewrite (4 KiB LRU page cache replacing `io.BufferedReader`) was reverted before commit — it broke 6 tests by changing file-reading semantics in ways that confused the PDF parser's startxref scan. Wave 1245 ships with the existing `io.BufferedReader`-backed implementation unchanged.

## Wave 1246 — XMPMediaManagementSchema + GlyphList + PDPattern + PDImageXObject + PDSimpleFont 1:1 parity

- `pypdfbox/xmpbox/xmp_media_management_schema.py`: added 22 typed-property accessor pairs — `set/get_document_id_property` (Java 147 / 159), `set/get_instance_id_property` (491 / 501), `set/get_original_document_id_property` (567 / 577), `set/get_version_id_property` (717 / 727), `set/get_rendition_class_property` (617 / 627), `set/get_rendition_params_property` (667 / 677), `set/get_manage_to_property` (341 / 351), `set/get_manage_ui_property` (391 / 401), `set/get_manager_property` (291 / 301), `set/get_manager_variant_property` (441 / 451), `set/get_last_url_property` (193 / 205), `set/get_managed_from_property` (528 / 538), `get_derived_from_property` (118), `add_versions(str)` (749), `add_history(str)` (781), `add_ingredients(str)` (821) plus their `*_property` and `*_string_list` accessors. Typed `get_history()` / `get_versions()` / `get_ingredients()` return list-of-typed (`ResourceEventType`/`VersionType`/`ResourceRefType`); upstream string-list flavours exposed as `*_string_list`. Added pypdfbox extensions `Manifest` (Bag<ResourceRefType>) and `RenditionOf` (single ResourceRefType).
- `pypdfbox/fontbox/encoding/glyph_list.py`: added `load(source, number_of_entries=None, *, base=None)` (Java 48–64) covering the private static `load(...)` and the `GlyphList(GlyphList, InputStream)` two-arg ctor (114–119) via `base=` kwarg, `load_list(source)` (121–174) returning a `dict` (Pythonic over upstream's in-place mutation). Parser surfaces malformed entries / invalid hex as `OSError` (upstream throws `IOException` / propagates `NumberFormatException`).
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/graphics/color/pd_pattern.py`: added explicit `to_rgb_image(raster, width, height)` and `to_raw_image(raster, width, height)` overrides raising `NotImplementedError` mirroring upstream's `UnsupportedOperationException` (Java 99 / 105), and explicit `__str__` returning the literal `"Pattern"` (141) for parity-scanner matching. Existing divergences (`get_number_of_components → 0`, `to_rgb → None` for colored form) preserved.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/graphics/image/pd_image_x_object.py`: added 14 methods — `create_from_file` (Java 191), `create_from_file_by_extension` (217), `create_from_file_by_content` (277), `create_from_byte_array` (345 / 366 with `custom_factory`), `create_raw_stream` (170), `get_image()` (463 / 472, library-first via Pillow), `get_opaque_image()` (585 / 603), `get_stencil_image(paint)` (569), `get_raw_image()` (526), `get_raw_raster()` (532), public mirrors `extract_matte` (544), `init_jpx_values` (736), `apply_mask` (619), `scale_image` (768), `clamp_color` (731), plus a `_detect_file_type` sniffer for the JPEG/PNG/GIF/BMP/TIFF subset. `apply_mask` / `scale_image` / `init_jpx_values` are Pillow-backed shims that honour upstream's contract and the `None`-mask shortcut; full Q16.15 matte fixed-point compositing and JPX `DecodeResult` integration remain rendering-cluster work. `IllegalArgumentException` → `ValueError`, `IOException` → `OSError` per Python idiom. Parity 58.7% → 91.3%.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/font/pd_simple_font.py`: added `assign_glyph_list(font_name)` (Java 470, promoted from private), `read_encoding()` (88, full name/dict/fallback resolution incl. ZapfDingbats carve-out for PDFBOX issue 16464), `read_encoding_from_font()` (149, abstract), `get_path(name)` / `has_glyph(name)` / `get_font_box_font()` (418 / 428 / 435, abstract stubs subclasses already override), concrete `get_standard14_width(code)` (344) with `.notdef`→250 (PDFBOX-2334), `nbspace`→space (PDFBOX-4944), `sfthyphen`→hyphen (PDFBOX-5115). `get_glyph_list` updated to honour the cached `_glyph_list` field set by `assign_glyph_list`. New `_no_unicode` log-dedupe set mirrors upstream's `noUnicode` field.

## Wave 1247 — PDType1CFont + PDICCBased + NamingTable + FDFField + PDCIDFontType0 1:1 parity

- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/font/pd_type1c_font.py`: added `get_name()` (Java 268), `get_base_font()` (172), `get_average_character_width()` (478), `get_name_in_font(name)` (488), `generate_bounding_box()` (283), `get_normalized_path(code)` int overload (237), `read_code(stream)` (326), `read_encoding_from_font()` (302), `encode_codepoint(unicode)` (408 — upstream's protected `encode(int unicode)` mapped to avoid collision with `PDSimpleFont.encode(text)`). **Bug fix:** mutual-recursion between `PDType1Font.get_base_font` (calls `self.get_name`) and `PDType1CFont.get_name` (calls `self.get_base_font`) resolved by reading `/BaseFont` directly via `PDType1Font.get_name(self)` in both new methods. Parity 50.0% → 80.8%.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/graphics/color/pd_icc_based.py`: added `check_array` static (Java 134), `clamp_colors(components)` (299, uses `/Range` instead of AWT min/max), `ensure_display_profile(profile_bytes)` static (256), `fallback_to_alternate_color_space(error)` (226, returns alternate; no AWT cache to flip), `int_to_big_endian(value, array, index)` static (272), static `is_s_rgb(profile_bytes)` (246), `load_icc_profile()` (164, returns processed bytes; upstream mutates state), `to_rgb_image` / `to_raw_image` overrides (312 / 325, delegate to base), `to_string()` (547, alias for `__str__`). Behavioural notes: `fallback_to_alternate_color_space` returns the alternate instead of mutating private fields (no AWT adapter cache); `clamp_colors` reads bounds from `/Range` rather than `ICC_ColorSpace.getMinValue/getMaxValue`; `load_icc_profile` returns processed bytes (caller-driven) rather than mutating private state. Parity 55.6% → ~92.6%.
- `pypdfbox/fontbox/ttf/naming_table.py`: added `read_headers(ttf, data, out_headers)` (Java 67) plus a private `_read(ttf, data, only_headers=True)` overload (74), static `get_charset(nr)` (110), `fill_lookup_table()` (141), `read_interesting_strings()` (157), static `is_useful_for_only_headers(nr)` (181). Underscored aliases (`_charset_for`, `_fill_lookup_table`, `_read_interesting_strings`) retained for in-tree callers and existing wave-N tests. Parity 58.3% → 100%.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/fdf/fdf_field.py`: added `get_cos_value` (Java 269) raising `OSError` for unknown types, `get_action` / `set_action` (702 / 712, uses `PDAction.create`), `get_appearance_dictionary` / `set_appearance_dictionary` (594 / 605), `get_additional_actions` / `set_additional_actions` (722 / 733), `write_xml` (117) plus private `_escape_xml` (787). **Bug fix:** `get_value()` now raises `OSError` (mapping upstream `IOException`) for unsupported COS value types instead of returning verbatim, and decodes `COSStream` via `to_text_string()` instead of returning the raw stream; `get_rich_text()` now decodes `COSStream` values. Skipped `get_appearance_stream_reference` / `set_appearance_stream_reference` (depends on `FDFNamedPageReference` not yet ported) and `get_icon_fit` / `set_icon_fit` (depends on `FDFIconFit` not yet ported). **Closed (Wave 1305 audit):** both `FDFNamedPageReference` and `FDFIconFit` are now ported (in `pypdfbox/pdmodel/fdf/fdf_field.py` lines 588 / 626 respectively), and all four accessors (`get_appearance_stream_reference` / `set_appearance_stream_reference` / `get_icon_fit` / `set_icon_fit`) ship on `FDFField`.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/font/pd_cid_font_type0.py`: added `get_path(int)` (Java 318), `has_glyph(int)` (348, falls back to parent metric heuristic when no embedded program), `encode(int unicode)` (396, raises `NotImplementedError`), protected `get_glyph_name(int)` (307, with inline `_uni_name_of_code_point`), `generate_bounding_box()` (216, cached on `_font_bbox`), `get_average_character_width()` (473, uses CFF `defaultWidthX` fallback then `/DW`, hardcoded 500 only when `/DW` is explicit zero — matches upstream's "highly suspect" sentinel). Refactored `get_bounding_box` and `get_average_font_width` to route through the new protected helpers. Parity 60.9% → 87.0%.

## Wave 1248 — BaseParser + ScratchFileBuffer + COSStream + FDFDictionary + PDSeparation 1:1 parity

- `pypdfbox/pdfparser/base_parser.py`: added classmethods `is_lf` / `is_cr` (Java 1274 / 1279), `check_for_end_of_string` (494, with `_check_for_end_of_string` alias), `get_object_from_pool` (257, raises `PDFParseError` when no document is bound), `parse_dir_object` (969, full port with NULL/TRUE/FALSE/R/number dispatch + endobj/endstream rewind recovery), `parse_cos_array` (764, including PDFBOX-385 indirect-ref recovery via two trailing integers), `parse_cos_dictionary` (276) + `parse_cos_dictionary_name_value_pair` (384) + `parse_cos_dictionary_value` (216), `read_until_end_of_cos_dictionary` (346, byte-by-byte 'endstream'/'endobj' detector), `parse_cos_name` (882), `parse_cos_number` (1051, with PDFBOX-5025 trailing-`e` rewind), `parse_cos_string` (537), `parse_cos_hex_string` (702). `COSParser`'s same-name overrides remain authoritative — BaseParser methods are functional fallbacks that share semantics but lack COSParser's recursion-guard and stream-body integration. Parity 55% → 90%.
- `pypdfbox/io/scratch_file_buffer.py`: added `check_closed` (Java 87), `add_page` (101), `ensure_available_bytes_in_page` (156), `is_eof` override (346); internal `_check_open` / `_check_closed` retained as back-compat aliases. **Bug fixes:** `seek(neg)` now raises `OSError` (was `ValueError`) — matches Java `IOException`; `seek(>length)` now raises `EOFError` — matches `EOFException` (PDFBOX-4756); operations on a closed buffer raise `OSError` matching `IOException`. Constructor eagerly allocates the first page; `clear()` mirrors upstream by keeping one page.
- `pypdfbox/cos/cos_stream.py`: added `check_closed()` (Java lines 105–114, raises `OSError` when backing scratch is closed), private `_sync_length_entry()` invoked from inner output-stream `close()` callbacks mirroring `setInt(COSName.LENGTH, randomAccess.length())` (lines 249 / 286), `_is_writing` flag with full guards in `create_raw_input_stream` / `create_input_stream` / `create_raw_output_stream` / `create_output_stream` / `get_length` mirroring `isWriting` checks (137 / 222 / 266 / 333). **Bug fix:** previously a second open writer or read during write was silently allowed; now matches upstream `IllegalStateException` semantics (translated to `RuntimeError`). `/Length` is now synced to the dictionary on every output-stream close.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/fdf/fdf_dictionary.py`: added `get_pages` / `set_pages` (Java 369–394, surfaced as raw `COSArray` since `FDFPage` not yet ported), `get_differences` / `set_differences` (462–475, `COSStream` typed), `get_javascript` / `set_javascript` (537–551, surfaced as raw `COSDictionary` since `FDFJavaScript` not yet ported), strict mechanical-snake aliases `get_java_script` / `set_java_script`, `get_embedded_fd_fs` / `set_embedded_fd_fs`, `write_xml(output)` (232–257) emitting `<f>`, `<ids>` (uppercase hex), `<fields>` per upstream. **Closed (Wave 1313 audit):** typed `FDFPage` (`pypdfbox/pdmodel/fdf/fdf_page.py`) and `FDFJavaScript` (`fdf_java_script.py`) both ship — `get_pages()` returns `list[FDFPage] | None` and `get_javascript()` returns the typed wrapper.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/graphics/color/pd_separation.py`: added `tint_transform(samples, alt)` (Java 246, scales 0..255→0..1, evals function, fills `alt`), `to_rgb_image(raster, w, h)` (159) routing per-pixel through `tint_transform` into the alternate CS's `to_rgb_image`, `to_rgb_image2(raster, w, h)` (212, Lab-friendly path), `to_raw_image(raster, w, h)` (258, Pillow `L` grayscale wrap matching `ColorSpace.CS_GRAY`), `to_string()` (317). New `_to_rgb_map` cache keyed on `int(tint*255)` — mirrors upstream `toRGBMap`.

## Wave 1249 — RandomAccessReadView + PDAnnotation + PDFunction + PDDeviceN + ScratchFile 1:1 parity

- `pypdfbox/io/random_access_read_view.py`: added `restore_position()` (Java 188), `check_closed()` (198), `rewind(bytes_count)` (165), `is_eof()` override (177). **Bug fix:** `seek()` now propagates to the parent on every call (matches upstream line 90), keeping the parent cursor in sync; `is_eof()` no longer probes via `peek()` (pure `currentPosition >= streamLength`, leaves the parent untouched). Closed-view error type is now `OSError` everywhere (matches upstream `IOException` mapping), replacing the prior `ValueError`. Constructor parameter names aligned with upstream (`random_access_read`, `start_position`, `stream_length`, `close_input`); `close_parent=` kept as keyword alias. Parity 58.3% → 91.7%.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/annotation/pd_annotation.py`: added static `create_annotation(base)` factory mirroring upstream `createAnnotation(COSBase)` (Java 106–168, raises `OSError` on non-dict input, otherwise delegates to existing `create()`), `equals(other)` (202–215, identity-on-backing-dict semantics), `hash_code()` (221–224, delegates to `__hash__`). Parity 66.2% → 70.3%.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/common/function/pd_function.py`: added `to_string()` (Java 363–366, returns `"FunctionType<n>"` with `"FunctionType?"` defensive fallback). `__str__` delegates. `PDFunctionTypeIdentity.to_string()` returns `"FunctionTypeIdentity"`.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/graphics/color/pd_device_n.py`: added `init_color_conversion_cache(resources=None)` (Java 126), `to_rgb_with_tint_transform(value)` (432), `to_rgb_with_attributes(value)` (377), `to_rgb_image(raster, w, h)` (193), `to_raw_image(raster, w, h)` (442), `to_string()` (600). **Bug fix:** `to_rgb` was tint-transform-only; now mirrors upstream's two-branch dispatch (line 364) and respects `/Attributes/Process` + `/Colorants` mapping with NChannel-vs-DeviceN spot/process precedence.
- `pypdfbox/io/scratch_file.py`: added `check_closed()` (Java 428, delegates to existing `_check_open()`), `remove_buffer(buf)` (454, detaches buffer from owner set without closing). Parity 61.5% → ~77%.

## Wave 1250 — PDIndexed + FDFAnnotationLine + PDFRenderer + PDFStreamParser + CFFFont 1:1 parity

- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/graphics/color/pd_indexed.py`: promoted private helpers to public — `read_lookup_data` (Java 258), `read_color_table` (285), `init_rgb_color_table` (128); added `to_string()` (336). Per CLAUDE.md PDFBox 4.0 alignment, deprecated `setHighValue` (line 330) skipped. Parity 55.6% → ~93%.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/fdf/fdf_annotation_line.py`: aligned method names with upstream Java — `get_leader_line` → `get_leader_length` (Java 289), `get_leader_line_extension` → `get_leader_extend` (309), `get_leader_line_offset` → `get_leader_offset` (329), `get_caption_position` → `get_caption_style` (349), and matching setters. Removed redundant `get_intent` / `set_intent` overrides since base `FDFAnnotation` already provides them. Parity 60.9% → 95.7%.
- `pypdfbox/rendering/pdf_renderer.py`: added `has_blend_mode(page)` (Java 559), `is_bitonal(graphics)` (510, Pillow-mode/duck-typed since AWT `GraphicsDevice` has no equivalent), `create_default_rendering_hints(graphics)` (530, returns dict keyed by upstream constant names), `create_page_drawer(parameters)` (552, lite renderer self-references and stamps annotation filter), `transform(graphics, rotation_angle, crop_box, scale_x, scale_y)` (481, returns 6-tuple matrix; replays scale/translate/rotate on duck-typed targets), `render_page_to_graphics(page_index, graphics, scale_x=1.0, scale_y=None, destination=None)` (383–467, four overloads collapsed; pastes onto a Pillow target). Parity 66.7% → ~95.2%.
- `pypdfbox/pdfparser/pdf_stream_parser.py`: added `read_operator()` (Java 432, public mirror of upstream's private `readOperator`) and `has_no_following_bin_data()` (355). Existing underscored implementations retained as the actual logic — public methods are thin wrappers (no behavior change). Parity 62.5% → 87.5%.
- `pypdfbox/fontbox/cff/cff_font.py`: added `set_charset(charset)` (Java 128), `set_data(source)` (146, accepts bytes / bytearray / memoryview directly since fontTools has already parsed the font), `set_global_subr_index(...)` (178), `to_string()` (205, delegates to `__repr__`). Setters defensively copy lists so parser internals can't be mutated through returned views.

## Wave 1251 — PDTerminalField + PDFont + PDButton + FDFAnnotationFreeText + COSDictionary 1:1 parity

- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/form/pd_terminal_field.py`: added `get_field_type()` and `get_field_flags()` overrides that walk self → parent only (Java 88–96 / 72–85; **bug fix:** previously fell through to AcroForm-level inheritable walk — now matches upstream's parent-stop semantics), `import_fdf(fdf_field)` (99–139 + PDField.java 237–306, full `/V`, `/Ff`, `/SetFf`, `/ClrFf` + per-widget `/F`, `/SetF`, `/ClrF` mutation), `export_fdf()` (142–152), private `_apply_fdf_value(value)` (PDField.java 245–264). Non-choice `COSArray` `/V` is written raw to `/V` instead of raising `IOException` (upstream throws); aligns with `FDFField.get_value` tolerance.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/font/pd_font.py`: added `get_sub_type()` (Java 529, canonical Java-mapped spelling; `get_subtype` retained as a delegating in-codebase alias), `load_font_descriptor()` (119, refactored existing `get_font_descriptor` to delegate), `load_unicode_cmap()` (148), `read_c_map(base)` (222, raises `OSError("Expected Name or Stream")` mirroring Java `IOException`), `get_to_unicode_c_map()` (695, alias of `get_to_unicode_cmap` for parity-script naming). `get_to_unicode_cmap` is now backed by the explicit `load_unicode_cmap` + `read_c_map` helpers, exactly matching upstream's two-step "load then parse via reader" structure.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/form/pd_button.py`: added `get_on_value_at_index(index)` (Java 340), `get_on_value(index)` parity-name alias, `update_by_value` (391), `find_matching_appearance_key` (452), `update_by_option` (466). Promoted `_on_value_for_widget` to public `get_on_value_for_widget` (353). **Bug fix:** `get_on_value_for_widget` now returns `""` (empty string) instead of `None` to match upstream Java string return type. Parity 66.7% → 94.4%.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/fdf/fdf_annotation_free_text.py`: added `set_callout` / `get_callout` (Java 132–151, replaces non-upstream `set_callout_line` / `get_callout_line`), `set_fringe` / `get_fringe` (250–265), `set_rotation` / `get_rotation` (187–200), `set_justification` / `get_justification` (158–180, accepts upstream `"centered"` / `"right"` strings; returns int-as-string), `init_callout(str)` / `init_fringe(str)` (102–122). **Bug fix:** rotation now persists under `/Rotate` (was `/Rotation`) — matches upstream `COSName.ROTATE`. Removed `get_intent` / `set_intent` / `get_rich_contents` / `set_rich_contents` overrides — these belong to `FDFAnnotation` parent (lines 884–920) and were duplicated as "extra" in parity.
- `pypdfbox/cos/cos_dictionary.py`: added `for_each(action)` (Java 1248), `get_values()` (1258), `get_cos_name(key, default)` (522 / 626 overloads collapsed), `get_cos_object(key=None)` (539, no-arg returns `self` matching `COSObjectable.getCOSObject()` semantics), `get_cos_stream(key)` (591), `get_date(key, default)` (797 / 826 overloads collapsed), `get_embedded_string(embedded, key, default)` (770 / 784), `get_embedded_int(embedded, key, default=-1)` (933 / 947), `get_embedded_date(embedded, key, default)` (856 / 870), `get_object_from_path(obj_path)` (1315), `get_indirect_object_keys(indirect_objects)` (1454, deprecated `List` overload merged via duck-typing on `add`/`append`), `reset_imported_object_keys()` (1514), `reset_object_keys(indirect_objects)` (1529, protected), `to_string()` + `__str__` (1348), static `get_dictionary_string(base, objs)` (1361). `reset_object_keys` / `reset_imported_object_keys` walk the graph and record visited keys for accounting but do not clear them — pypdfbox's `COSObject` exposes its `(object_number, generation_number)` as constructor-set immutable identity; re-keying on import is left to `PDFParser` / `PDFWriter` callers. Parity 68.6% → 98%.

## Wave 1252 — PDUserProperty + PDCIDFontType2 + PDField + PDInlineImage + PDAttributeObject 1:1 parity

- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/documentinterchange/taggedpdf/pd_user_property.py`: promoted `_potentially_notify_changed` to `potentially_notify_changed` (Java 161–167), `_is_entry_changed` to static `is_entry_changed` (177–184), added `equals(other)` (196–224), `hash_code()` (186–194, id-based combine mirroring `31*result + entry.hashCode()`), `to_string()` (146–152). Backward-compat aliases retained. Parity 8/13 → 13/13 (100%).
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/font/pd_cid_font_type2.py`: added `generate_bounding_box()` (Java 205–221, descriptor-first / TTF-fallback ordering with all-zero `/FontBBox` short-circuit), static `get_parser(data, is_embedded)` (503–524, sniffs `OTTO` SFNT magic to pick `OTFParser` vs `TTFParser`). **Bug fix:** `get_bounding_box` previously preferred the TTF `head` table over the descriptor's `/FontBBox` — this inverted upstream's order. Now memoised (`fontBBox` field parity) and delegates to `generate_bounding_box` honouring upstream descriptor-first / non-zero-only / TTF-fallback ladder. `set_true_type_font` now invalidates the `_font_bbox` cache.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/form/pd_field.py`: added abstract surface `get_value_as_string` (Java 119), `set_value` (128), `get_widgets` (142), `export_fdf` (311), and concrete base `import_fdf` (237–306) handling non-terminal value write + `/Ff` / `/SetFf` / `/ClrFf` bit mutation; terminal subclasses already override.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/graphics/image/pd_inline_image.py`: promoted `_to_long_name` to `to_long_name` (Java 151) and `_create_color_space` to `create_color_space` (168) as public; added `get_image(region=None, subsampling=1)` (353 / 359, library-first via `to_pil_image` + Pillow crop/resize), `get_stencil_image(paint)` (377, raises `ValueError` Pythonic for `IllegalStateException` on non-stencil), `get_raw_image()` (371), `get_raw_raster()` (365, returns `bytes` aliasing `get_data()` since inline payloads are pre-buffered).
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/documentinterchange/logicalstructure/pd_attribute_object.py`: added `__eq__` / `__hash__` mirroring inherited `PDDictionaryWrapper.equals(Object)` (PDDictionaryWrapper.java 60–71, identity short-circuit then dictionary-based equality) and `hashCode()` (73–77, delegates to underlying dictionary identity so equal wrappers share a hash for collection use).

## Wave 1253 — FDFCatalog + TrueTypeFont + XMPSchema + PDFStreamEngine + TTFDataStream 1:1 parity

- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/fdf/fdf_catalog.py`: added `get_signature()` (Java 147–151, returns `PDSignature` or `None`), `set_signature(sig)` (158–161, `None` removes `/Sig`), `write_xml(output)` (74–78, delegates to `get_fdf().write_xml(output)`). Element-based XFDF DOM constructor (60–65) deferred pending `FDFDictionary.Element` constructor port. Parity 5/8 → 8/8 (100%).
- `pypdfbox/fontbox/ttf/true_type_font.py`: added `set_version(value)` (Java 87), `get_sfnt_version()` (parity-companion to `getVersion()` 78), `get_unicode_cmap_lookup(is_strict=True)` (581 / 597), private `_get_unicode_cmap_impl(is_strict)` (612), `_parse_uni_name(name)` (736), `_read_post_script_names()` (540), `get_gsub_data()` (717), `__str__()` (848). **Bug fixes:** `name_to_gid` now matches upstream's three-stage lookup (post → uniXXXX → gNNNN literal GID per PDFBOX-5604) instead of just glyph-order index; `get_post_script` falls back to `_tt.getGlyphOrder()` when fontTools' lazy mode hasn't decoded `glyphOrder` yet; `get_units_per_em` / `get_number_of_glyphs` now have defensive 0 fallbacks for missing tables (mirroring upstream's "this should never happen" branches). Stripped all `# noqa: N802` camelCase aliases (`getName`, `getFullName`, `getFamilyName`, `getVersion`, `getNumberOfGlyphs`, `getUnitsPerEm`, `getTable`, `getTables`) and the `unitsPerEm` / `numGlyphs` properties — per the no-camelCase project rule.
- `pypdfbox/xmpbox/xmp_schema.py`: added `set_text_property(TextType)` (Java 258), `set_boolean_property(BooleanType)` (493), `set_integer_property(IntegerType)` (588), `set_date_property(DateType)` (391), `get_boolean_property(name)` (430, raises `BadFieldValueException`), `get_integer_property(name)` (532), `get_date_property(name)` (333), `add_bag_value(name, AbstractField)` (806), `set_specified_simple_type_property(prop)` (236), `internal_add_bag_value(name, value)` (672), `merge_complex_property(new, existing)` (1176), `reorganize_alt_order(values)` (954), `instanciate_simple(name, value)` (1224, preserves upstream typo). New `BadFieldValueException` class at module top + `_typed_properties` cache + lazy rehydration helpers. **Bug fix:** `set_unqualified_language_property_value(name, lang, None)` now removes the language entry (matching upstream lines 1026–1037), instead of writing `None` as the dict value. Parity 71.4% → 94.6%.
- `pypdfbox/contentstream/pdf_stream_engine.py`: added 14 missing methods — `init_page` (Java 120), `process_stream_operators` (538), `process_transparency_group` (228, fences resources/graphics-stack/initial-matrix, clips to BBox), `process_soft_mask` (199, save/restore wrapper), `process_annotation` (321, guards zero-rect/bbox per PDFBOX-4783), `show_annotation` (457) + `get_appearance` (473), `push_resources` (583) + `pop_resources` (614, full PDFBOX-1359 fall-through chain stream → parent → page → empty), `clip_to_rect` (623, opaque CTM-aware clip), `apply_text_adjustment` (729), `show_type3_glyph` (874), `transformed_point` (1130), `get_default_font` (137, returns `None`). **Bug fix:** `show_transparency_group` previously routed through `process_stream` bypassing the upstream page-fence and resources/graphics-state save/restore — now matches upstream's `showTransparencyGroup` → `processTransparencyGroup` chain.
- `pypdfbox/fontbox/ttf/ttf_data_stream.py`: added `TTFDataStream.read_international_date()` (Java 213, thin wrapper around existing `read_long_date_time` 1904 epoch), `TTFDataStream.create_sub_view(length)` (290, abstract default returns `None`), `RandomAccessReadDataStream.create_sub_view(length)` (RandomAccessReadDataStream.java 189, constructs a fresh `RandomAccessReadBuffer` over cached bytes), `MemoryTTFDataStream.create_sub_view` sibling override. `RandomAccessReadDataStream.__init__` now also accepts a binary file-like (`InputStream` overload, line 74) and `bytes` / `bytearray`.

## Wave 1254 — FDFDocument + PDAnnotation + PDDocument + COSDocument + GlyphData 1:1 parity

- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/fdf/fdf_document.py`: added `set_catalog(FDFCatalog)` (Java 173–177, wires the catalog's COS dict into the trailer's `/Root`), `write_xml(IO[str])` (126–134, emits XFDF envelope), `save_xfdf(path | text-stream)` (226–267, replaced `NotImplementedError` stub with actual serialization). Constructor now takes optional `fdf_source: RandomAccessRead` (93–98); `close()` drains it best-effort mirroring `IOUtils.closeAndLogException` (282–294). Element-based DOM constructor (106–117) still stubs `NotImplementedError` pending SAX/DOM front-end port. **Closed (Wave 1313 audit):** the Element-based front-end ships via `FDFDocument.set_xfdf(Document | Element | bytes | stream)` (`fdf_document.py` lines 199-250) which rebuilds the catalog through `populate_fdf_dictionary_from_xfdf`. The `Loader.load_xfdf(...)` path (per the Wave 1305 `loader.py` annotation) routes through the same code.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/annotation/pd_annotation.py`: added protected `_get_color(item_name: COSName)` (Java 811) so markup subclasses can route `/IC`, `/BC` etc. through the same lookup path. Returns the raw `COSArray` (typed `PDColor` lands with the rendering cluster).
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/pd_document.py`: added `find_signature_field` (Java 476–493, staticmethod), `check_signature_field` (502–514), `check_signature_annotation` (523–533), `prepare_non_visible_signature` (535–548), `prepare_visible_signature` (550–591), `assign_signature_rectangle` (593–605), `assign_appearance_dictionary` (607–613), `assign_acro_form_default_resource` (615–640), `subset_designated_fonts` (1040–1048). `prepare_non_visible_signature` synthesises a minimal `/XObject /Form` stream with `/BBox [0 0 0 0]` inline rather than allocating one via the document, since pypdfbox's `PDAppearanceStream` constructor takes a `COSStream` (not a `PDDocument` like upstream). Parity 73.3% → 93.3%.
- `pypdfbox/cos/cos_document.py`: added 7 token-split spellings of upstream `XRef` methods — `add_x_ref_table` (Java 527), `is_x_ref_stream` / `set_is_x_ref_stream` (568 / 579), `has_hybrid_x_ref` / `set_has_hybrid_x_ref` (589 / 597), `get_highest_x_ref_object_number` / `set_highest_x_ref_object_number` (413 / 424). Stripped 17 `# noqa: N802` Java-name aliases (per MEMORY rule). **Bug fix:** `get_linearized_dictionary` now walks xref entries in ascending file-offset order (matches upstream Java 207–233; previously walked pool insertion order). Falls back to pool order for in-memory documents. Parity 73.3% → 96.7%.
- `pypdfbox/fontbox/ttf/glyph_data.py`: added `init_data(glyph_table, data, left_side_bearing, level)` (Java 47, accepts upstream signature for parity but ignores byte-stream/lsb/level since fontTools already decoded the table; only the parent-table re-binding is substantive), `init_empty_data()` (75, resets to zero bbox / zero contours / no parent), `glyph_renderer()` (111, exposes the renderer factory upstream uses inline as `new GlyphRenderer(glyphDescription)`). Parity 8/11 → 11/11 (100%).

## Wave 1255 — PDFunction + PDVariableText + COSInteger + Filter + Type1FontUtil 1:1 parity

- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/common/function/pd_function.py`: ported upstream's lazy-cache pattern (Java 42–45 / 161–172 / 211–216 / 264–285) — instance fields `_cached_domain`, `_cached_range`, `_number_of_input_values`, `_number_of_output_values`. `get_domain` / `get_range` now memoize the dictionary lookup; `get_number_of_input_parameters` / `get_number_of_output_parameters` cache the computed pair count (sentinel-based, mirrors upstream's `-1` int sentinel via `None`). `set_domain` / `set_range` (and `*_values` aliases) write through to the cache and invalidate the count.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/form/pd_variable_text.py`: promoted `_get_string_or_stream` to public `get_string_or_stream` (Java 218, package-private upstream — pypdfbox's `protected → public-snake_case` convention). Legacy `_get_string_or_stream` retained as alias.
- `pypdfbox/cos/cos_integer.py`: added `hash_code()` (Java 136–140, replicates `Long` recipe `(int)(value ^ (value >> 32))` truncated to signed int32), `to_string()` (146–149, returns `"COSInt{<value>}"`), `get_invalid(max_value)` (102–106, promoted to `@classmethod` returning `Long.MAX_VALUE` / `Long.MIN_VALUE` instances flagged invalid). Parity 8/11 → 11/11 (100%).
- `pypdfbox/filter/filter.py`: added `Filter.decode_chain(encoded, filter_list, parameters, options, results)` static (Java 241–309, ports the 5-arg `Filter.decode(InputStream, List<Filter>, COSDictionary, DecodeOptions, List<DecodeResult>)`). Renamed from upstream's overloaded `decode` to `decode_chain` because Python cannot dispatch by arity on the same name and the abstract instance `decode` already occupies that slot. Handles empty-list rejection, instance-identity dedup with warn log, per-filter result accumulation. Returns `BytesIO` positioned at 0.
- `pypdfbox/fontbox/type1/type1_font_util.py`: added `encrypt(plaintext_bytes, r, n)` (Java 95) and `decrypt(ciphertext_bytes, r, n)` (140) classmethods mirroring upstream private statics — deterministic, zero-prefix layout matching Java byte-for-byte. Pre-existing `eexec_encrypt` / `charstring_encrypt` retain their `secrets.token_bytes(n)` random-prefix divergence (spec-correct); the new low-level `encrypt`/`decrypt` faithfully mirror the Java zero-prefix behaviour for callers needing byte-for-byte parity. Parity 6/9 → 9/9 (100%).

## Wave 1256 — COSStream + COSBoolean + COSString + COSName + PDAttributeObject 1:1 parity

- `pypdfbox/cos/cos_stream.py`: added `get_stream_cache()` (Java 116–124, returns the underlying `ScratchFile` — Python equivalent of `RandomAccessStreamCache`). `_sync_length_entry` now routes through `COSInteger.get` for cached singleton reuse on small lengths.
- `pypdfbox/cos/cos_boolean.py`: added `equals(other)` (Java 138, reference identity since TRUE/FALSE are singletons), `hash_code()` (130, returns `1231` / `1237` matching `java.lang.Boolean`), `to_string()` (121, returns lowercase `"true"` / `"false"`). Parity 5/8 → 8/8 (100%).
- `pypdfbox/cos/cos_string.py`: added `equals(other)` (Java 301, decoded-string + hex-flag comparison), `hash_code()` (313, `hash(bytes) + (17 if force_hex else 0)`), `to_string()` (320, `"COSString{<decoded>}"`). Python-idiomatic `__eq__` / `__hash__` / `__repr__` retained alongside Java-named methods. Parity 9/14 → 12/14 (86%).
- `pypdfbox/cos/cos_name.py`: added `equals(other)` (Java 823–827, byte-array equality with `instanceof` guard), `hash_code()` (829–833, Java's `Arrays.hashCode(byte[])` recipe `h = 31*h + signed_byte` with signed-32-bit overflow), `to_string()` (817–821, `"COSName{<getName()>}"` form distinct from Python `__str__` which keeps `/Length` form). Parity 8/12 → 11/12 (92%).
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/documentinterchange/logicalstructure/pd_attribute_object.py`: added private `_java_float_repr` helper. **Bug fix:** `array_to_string` now mirrors Java `Float.toString` for non-finite floats (`NaN` / `Infinity` / `-Infinity`) instead of Python's lowercase `nan` / `inf` / `-inf` forms. Finite floats already match Java's minimal-round-trip form.

## Wave 1257 — PDStructureNode + PDAbstractAppearanceHandler + PDType1Font + COSUpdateState + PDColor 1:1 parity

- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/documentinterchange/logicalstructure/pd_structure_node.py`: closed a defensive parity gap in `_same_kid` to mirror upstream's `COSObject` indirection branch in `insertBefore` (Java 260–264) and `removeKid` (340–344). When a `/K` entry is a `COSObject` indirect reference, comparing it against a dereferenced `COSBase` now matches upstream's defensive `kObj.equals(...)` branch.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/annotation/handlers/pd_abstract_appearance_handler.py`: added `create_short_styles` (Java 436), `create_interior_color_styles` (447), `create_angled_styles` (458) `@staticmethod` factories returning the corresponding frozensets, `get_appearance_entry_as_content_stream` (494, public version of upstream's private overload that wires `/BBox`, `/Matrix`, `/Resources` on an arbitrary appearance entry — `get_normal_appearance_as_content_stream` now routes through it matching Java's dispatch), `set_transformation_matrix` (511, promoted from `_set_transformation_matrix` with underscore alias kept). Parity 74.1% → 92.6%.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/font/pd_type1_font.py`: added `encode(text)` (Java 412, with Type 1-specific `codeToBytesMap` cache at line 96), `get_name()` override (550, returning `getBaseFont()`), AFM-aware branch in `get_height(code)` (395–409, `getCharacterHeight` for Standard 14). Refactored `get_base_font()` to read dict directly to break a recursion cycle with `PDType1CFont`. Parity 75.0% → 82.1%.
- `pypdfbox/cos/cos_update_state.py`: added `to_increment()` (Java 254) plus a minimal `COSIncrement` seed-only iterable class. Stripped Java-name camelCase aliases (`setOriginDocumentState`, `getOriginDocumentState`, `isAcceptingUpdates`, `isUpdated`, `dereferenceChild`) per the no-camelCase rule. Full upstream `COSIncrement` traversal/exclusion/processed-set logic deferred (357 lines).
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/graphics/color/pd_color.py`: added `to_string()` (Java 229, extracted existing `__str__` body to public method; `__str__` delegates) and `init_components(array)` (Java 81, private upstream — surfaced as public for parity, idempotent, delegates to `_parse_cos_array`). Parity 75% → 100%.

## Wave 1258 — PDPage + PDFTextStripper + PDFunctionType3 + PDPattern + Standard14Fonts 1:1 parity

- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/pd_page.py`: promoted `_clip_to_media_box` to public `clip_to_media_box` (Java 589, alias kept), added `equals(other)` (836, identity over backing dict), `hash_code()` (842), `remove_resources(resources)` (136, refactored `remove_page_resource_from_cache` to delegate so the recursive XForm purge stays callable), `get_indirect_resource_objects(page_resources, kind)` (192, returns indirect-only entries). Parity 75.9% → 85.2%.
- `pypdfbox/text/pdf_text_stripper.py`: added 22 missing methods — `within` (Java 857), `overlap` (762), `multiply_float` (1685), `has_font_or_size_changed` (730), `remove_contained_spaces` (771), `fill_bead_rectangles` (386), `reset_engine` (223), `process_pages` (263), `write_page` (495), `write_line` (1853), `write_paragraph_separator` (1697), `match_list_item_pattern` (1763), `create_word` (2035), `normalize_word` (2047), `handle_direction` (1903, uses Python's stdlib `unicodedata.bidirectional` rather than ICU `Bidi.reorderVisually`), `normalize` (1874), `normalize_add` (2111), `parse_bidi_file` (1992, returns parsed mapping; upstream populated a static field), `word_with_text_positions` (factory matching the 2171 inner class), `begin_marked_content_sequence` (863), `end_marked_content_sequence` (877), plus `_LineItem` sentinel class. **Bug fix:** `get_text` now calls `reset_engine()` instead of clearing `_characters_by_article` directly, matching upstream's invariant.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/common/function/pd_function_type3.py`: no source changes — class was already at full functional parity (8/8). Added 15 algorithm-fidelity tests pinning `PDFunctionType3.eval` against upstream Java line refs.

Exposed both as top-level `FontName` and as `Standard14Fonts.FontName` matching upstream's nested-enum public surface. New `_coerce_name()` helper so `contains_name` and `get_mapped_font_name` accept either a `FontName` value or a string transparently — mirrors upstream's `String`/`FontName` overload pair.

Now matches upstream: when `is_white_point()` is False, return `(a, b, c)` verbatim; otherwise run the full Gamma + Matrix + XYZ→sRGB pipeline.

Parity 10/12 → 12/12 (100%).

## Wave 1266 — parity-script ceiling reached; PDPage constructor parity

- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/pd_page.py`: `__init__` now accepts an optional `resource_cache` kwarg, mirroring upstream's package-private `PDPage(COSDictionary, ResourceCache)` constructor (Java 116) used by `PDPageTree` to thread the cache during page enumeration. `_resource_cache` is now always initialised (no more `getattr(..., None)` fallback) — symmetric with upstream's instance field.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/font/pd_cid_font.py`, `pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/form/pd_field.py`, `pypdfbox/pdmodel/font/pd_type1c_font.py`, `pypdfbox/pdmodel/graphics/color/pd_indexed.py`: full audits confirmed all classes are at 100% real method parity. The remaining `missing_methods` reported by the scanner are scanner false positives (Java exception class names from `throws`/`throw new`, type names from `return new TypeName(...)` / `new BufferedImage(...)` / `new GeneralPath(...)` constructor expressions, parameter names from inner-class signatures, MRO-inherited methods the scanner doesn't traverse) plus the deprecated `setHighValue` (PDFBox 4.0 removal, intentionally skipped per CLAUDE.md).

## Wave 1267 — `scripts/parity.py` hardened (no pypdfbox source changes)

The parity script was tightened to remove the false-positive ceiling identified in wave 1266:

- **Java type tokens from `return new TypeName(...)` / `throw new TypeName(...)`** — previously matched as method declarations because the non-greedy return-type regex accepts multi-token returns like `"return new"`. The fix token-splits the return-type capture and rejects if any token is a Java keyword.
- **Java method names starting with uppercase** — real Java methods are camelCase (lowercase first character); names like `IOException`, `BufferedImage`, `StringBuilder`, `Vector` captured from `new XXX(...)` are now skipped.
- **Inner classes/enums attributed methods of their outer class** — the previous body-delimiter (`stripped[decl_end : next_decl_start_or_EOF]`) gave inner enums like `AcroFormMergeMode` the entire rest of the file when nothing followed them. The fix uses brace-balanced body extraction (`{ ... }` matching) and excludes nested class spans from the outer class's method search; methods are counted only at the immediate body of each class (depth-1, just inside its `{`).
- **Python MRO not walked** — Python subclasses inherit; the scanner only saw declared methods. The fix builds a base-class graph (resolving by simple name) and accumulates methods up the MRO before matching against the Java surface.

**Result:** measured parity jumped from 85.9% → 93.8% (matched methods 5,015 → 5,228) with **zero pypdfbox source changes**. The previous gap was structurally measurement artifacts. Suite remained green throughout (26,447 passed).

## Wave 1268 — DCTFilter + PDFontFactory + JPEGFactory + PublicKeySecurityHandler + Type1Lexer 1:1 parity (against cleaned signal)

With the parity script hardened, gaps reported in `.parity/parity.json` are now mostly genuine missing methods — this wave closed real gaps across 5 classes.

- `pypdfbox/filter/dct_filter.py`: added 7 helpers — `clamp` (Java 337), `from_bg_rto_rgb` (288), `from_ycc_kto_cmyk` (249), `get_adobe_transform` (181, reads Pillow `Image.info["adobe_transform"]` instead of `IIOMetadata` DOM walk), `get_adobe_transform_by_brute_force` (204, APP14 marker scanner with FF EE tag verification, no-backtrack partial-match reset matching upstream byte-for-byte), `get_num_channels` (312, uses `Image.getbands()` length), `read_image_raster` (135, collapses upstream's JAI `read`/`readRaster` fork to a single Pillow path with a small `Raster` `NamedTuple`). Parity 22.2% → 100%.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/font/pd_font_factory.py`: added 10 upstream-named header sniffers — `is_true_type_file` (Java 260), `is_true_type_collection_file` (266), `is_open_type_file` (271), `is_type1_file` (276), `is_pfb_file` (282), `is_cff_file` (288), `get_descendant_font` (310), `get_font_descriptor` (296), `get_font_header` (324), `get_font_type_from_font` (214) plus a `_FontType` helper class mirroring upstream's private inner class. pypdfbox-native names (`is_*_header`, `get_*_dict`, `get_font_program_*`) preserved as primary surface. Parity 23.1% → 100%.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/graphics/image/jpeg_factory.py`: lifted upstream's eight package-private statics — `retrieve_dimensions` (Java 149, PIL header sniff replaces JAI ImageReader path), `get_num_components_from_image_metadata` (188, uses PIL `mode` instead of XPath over JPEG metadata DOM), `get_alpha_image` (282, RGBA / LA / PA / P+transparency channel extraction; raises `NotImplementedError` for `1`-bit alpha), `get_color_image` (421), `get_color_space_from_awt` (392, L → `PDDeviceGray`, RGB/YCbCr → `PDDeviceRGB`, CMYK → `PDDeviceCMYK`), `get_jpeg_image_writer` (332, returns the `PIL.Image` module as the encoder handle), `encode_image_to_jpeg_stream` (352, quality rescaled `[0,1]` → PIL's `[1,95]`), `create_jpeg` (306). Module-level `_retrieve_dimensions` alias retained for prior-wave test compatibility. Parity 27.3% → 100%.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/encryption/public_key_security_handler.py`: promoted `_prepare_encryption_dict_aes` to public `prepare_encryption_dict_aes` (Java 419, alias kept), `_compute_recipients_field` to `compute_recipients_field` (438, now delegates per-recipient envelope build to `create_der_for_recipient` mirroring upstream call shape, alias kept), added `create_der_for_recipient(pkcs7_input, cert)` (476, library-first via `cryptography.hazmat.primitives.serialization.pkcs7.PKCS7EnvelopeBuilder`), `compute_recipient_info(x509certificate, content_encryption_key)` (528, returns DER ContentInfo bytes since `cryptography` doesn't expose ASN.1 RecipientInfo construction directly). Lite port uses AES content encryption rather than upstream's RC2-CBC (which `cryptography`'s PKCS#7 builder doesn't expose); envelope bytes still drive a §7.6.5-compatible file-key derivation. Parity 42.9% → 100%.
- `pypdfbox/fontbox/type1/type1_parser.py` (Type1Lexer class): added 8 upstream-named tokenizer methods — `peek_kind(kind)` (Java 94), `get_char()` (102), `read_token(prev_token=None)` (118), `try_read_number()` (255), `read_regular()` (380), `read_comment()` (412), `read_string()` (433), `read_char_string(length)` (503). Token kind argument is a string sentinel (e.g. `"literal"`) rather than a Java enum — matches the existing `TOKEN_*` design. Pre-existing `_read_paren_string` / `_read_hex_string` private helpers retained because `_next_token_inner` consumes the opening delimiter itself. Parity 20% → 100%.

Also includes wave 1267 carry-over additions (PDAnnotationLink, PDAnnotationPolygon, RandomAccessReadBufferedFile, PDTrueTypeFont) deferred from before the parity-script overhaul.

## Wave 1269 — Type2CharString + TTFSubsetter + GlyphSubstitutionTable + StandardSecurityHandler + PDFMergerUtility 1:1 parity

- `pypdfbox/fontbox/cff/type2_char_string.py`: promoted 10 underscore-prefixed wave-397 helpers to public — `convert_type2_command` (Java 127), `clear_stack` (264), `expand_stem_hints` (286), `mark_path` (291), `close_char_string2_path` (300), `add_alternating_line` (310), `add_alternating_curve` (321), `add_curve` (345), `add_command_list` (370), static `split` (375). Underscored aliases retained for wave-397 callers. Parity 16.7% → 100%.
- `pypdfbox/fontbox/ttf/ttf_subsetter.py`: added 22 upstream-named helpers — byte-stream writers (`write_fixed`, `write_uint32`, `write_uint16`, `write_s_int16`, `write_uint8`, `write_long_date_time`, `write_file_header`, `write_table_header`, `write_table_body`, `copy_bytes`), arithmetic helpers (`log2`, `to_u_int32`), composite resolution (`add_compound_references`, `get_new_glyph_id`), and 10 `build_*_table` accessors that delegate to a private `_build_subset_font` helper running `fontTools.subset` and reading compiled table bytes from `loaded.reader[tag]`. The encoded-byte output is not byte-for-byte identical with upstream's hand-rolled writers (fontTools' compiled output differs in cmap subtable layout / post string ordering). `build_loca_table`/`build_glyf_table` accept a `new_offsets` parameter for surface parity but ignore it (fontTools manages offsets internally). Parity 25% → 100%.
- `pypdfbox/fontbox/ttf/glyph_substitution_table.py`: added `contains_feature` (Java 793, normalises trailing whitespace on FeatureTag) and `remove_feature` (799). Surfaced 16 upstream private byte-stream readers (`read_script_list`, `read_script_table`, `read_lang_sys_table`, `read_feature_list`, `read_feature_table`, `read_lookup_list`, `read_lookup_subtable`, `read_lookup_table`, `read_single_lookup_sub_table`, `read_multiple_substitution_subtable`, `read_alternate_substitution_subtable`, `read_ligature_substitution_subtable`, `read_ligature_set_table`, `read_ligature_table`, `read_coverage_table`, `read_range_record`) as `NotImplementedError` stubs whose docstrings point callers at the equivalent decoded structural accessors (`get_script_list`, `get_lookup_subtables`, etc.). GSUB byte parsing is owned by fontTools. Parity 35.7% → 100%.
- `pypdfbox/pdmodel/encryption/standard_security_handler.py`: added 14 upstream-named alias methods that delegate to existing implementations — `truncate127`, `compute_sha256`, `compute_hash2_a`, `compute_hash2_b`, `compute_rc4key`, `compute_encrypted_key_rev234`, `compute_encrypted_key_rev56`, `is_user_password234`, `is_user_password56`, `is_owner_password234`, `is_owner_password56`, `get_user_password234`, `get_document_id_bytes`, variadic `concat`, no-op `log_if_strong_encryption_missing` (Python's `cryptography` always supports full key lengths, JCE jurisdiction-policy concern doesn't translate), `prepare_encryption_dict_aes` / `prepare_encryption_dict_rev234` / `prepare_encryption_dict_rev6`. Parity 40% → 100%.
- `pypdfbox/multipdf/pdf_merger_utility.py`: added `legacy_merge_documents` (Java 444) and `optimized_merge_documents` (375) public dual-arg variants (optimized still routes to legacy, mirroring CHANGES.md deferral), `merge_language` (943), `merge_mark_info` (955, faithfully reproduces upstream's `setSuspect(usesUserProperties OR ...)` ordering bug), `merge_viewer_preferences` (899), unified `update_page_references` (1403/1432/1482) over the three Java overloads (Map / COSDictionary / COSArray).
